## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Docket:
  ## Character
  
  Design landing — multiple spec files, handbook, and paddock must end
  agreeing on a new verb decomposition. Spec-internal consistency is the
  load-bearing property; not a mechanical sweep.
  
  ## Docket
  
  Introduce `busn_fenestrate` as a new jurisdiction verb. Targets
  Windows OpenSSH only (hardens its `sshd_config`), uniform (no
  shell-letter), tabtarget `buw-jpF` (capital F = persistent config
  application; takes BURP investiture as param1), implementation seat
  shared with garrison in `bujb_jurisdiction.sh`. Locked design:
  
  - Two-phase remote-run ceremony forced by the fact that
    `Restart-Service sshd` terminates its own ssh session. Phase 1
    ends with Restart-Service as its final atomic op; phase 2
    reconnects via key auth for any post-state verification. Single
    ssh exec for the restart; bash orchestrator treats the disconnect
    immediately after that exec as the expected phase boundary.
  - `sshd -t` validation is the penultimate atomic op of phase 1
    (immediately before Restart-Service), so a malformed config
    aborts fenestrate before it could brick the running sshd.
  - Posture A — fenestrate writes sshd_config, that is its job.
    Verify-after-write follows the show-raw-then-bash-parse pattern:
    PowerShell `Get-Content` returns raw file bytes; bash-side
    BCG-compliant matching extracts effective directive values. Keeps
    PowerShell out of the parsing business (its error semantics are
    unreliable).
  - Manual password is needed only for the phase-1 initial ssh
    session (handbook precondition: `PasswordAuthentication yes`);
    everything after is automated key auth.
  
  Add BUSJPF subdoc carrying the two-phase operation contract; update
  mapping section + tabtarget catalog (13 → 14).
  
  Narrow `busn_garrison` to workload provisioning only — admin-trust
  front-half moves to fenestrate. Garrison never reads or writes any
  sshd_config; no verification gates. (Fenestrate hardens Windows
  OpenSSH only; for c/w garrisons, Cygwin/WSL sshd hardening is
  operator-manual; for b garrison, Linux/Mac sshd is operator-manual
  likewise.) Garrison's first natural action is admin SSH via key
  auth — failure there surfaces immediately. Update BUSJGB/C/W to
  reflect the workload-only contract. Garrison stays per-shell-letter;
  fenestrate is uniform.
  
  Rename `buw-jpg[bcw]` → `buw-jpG[bcw]` for consistency with
  `buw-jpF`'s capital-letter-for-persistent-config convention (garrison
  is also persistent/destructive; the existing lowercase `g` was
  mis-cased). Spec-only rename — no tabtargets exist on disk yet.
  Discovery recipe for references: `grep -rn "buw-jpg" Tools/buk/`.
  
  Sweep BUS0 drift sites that the dual-purpose pivot left and that
  fenestrate now honestly covers. Discovery recipe (candidates, not
  guaranteed fixes — judge each):
  `grep -rn "out-of-band\|no password fallback\|operator-bootstrapped" Tools/buk/`.
  Reframe §Garrison-Destructive Model paragraph (fenestrate carved off
  admin trust; garrison still subsumes workload lifecycle with no
  separate materialize/remove/enumerate verbs).
  
  Update `buhj_jurisdiction.sh` post-bootstrap hand-off to "Run
  Fenestrate, then Garrison." Linux/Mac stays operator-manual via
  ssh-copy-id handbook line (no fenestrate equivalent). Restructure
  paddock §Current Concept around the two-verb split (the existing
  unified-9-step-ceremony narrative no longer matches either verb
  individually — additive editing won't suffice). Split ceremony
  section into §Fenestrate ceremony + §Garrison ceremony. Adjust
  standing notes that no longer match.
  
  Done when:
  - BUS0 reflects the split with no contradicting prose anywhere
  - BUSJPF + revised BUSJGB/C/W carry their respective contracts
  - Tabtarget catalog has 14 entries with `buw-jpF` added and
    `buw-jpg[bcw]` renamed to `buw-jpG[bcw]` everywhere referenced
  - Handbook and paddock no longer claim garrison handles admin trust
  - `bujb_jurisdiction` body acknowledges housing both verbs
  
  Out of scope: any code (implementation is `implement-jurisdiction`'s
  job); PowerShell garrison + dispatch (deferred per paddock); adopt
  verb (deferred); end-to-end operator test on a Windows node.

ddb9fd9f  ₢A-AAo    Add 'Enable Password Authentication in sshd_config' step to Windows handbook procedure (between Start/Enable Service and Allow Firewall Port). Empirical finding from rocket bootstrap: Windows OpenSSH 9.5 ships with sshd_config in a state that blocks password auth — the wire protocol advertises keyboard-interactive (the standard wrapper for password delivery on Windows) but the server refuses to actually prompt, producing 'Permission denied (publickey,keyboard-interactive)' with no prompt ever shown to the operator. Microsoft Learn confirms: on Windows OpenSSH the only configurable AuthenticationMethods are 'password' and 'publickey' (KbdInteractiveAuthentication is in the not-available list); the server's PasswordAuthentication directive controls both wire methods. Default-shipped state empirically rejects password auth even when the wire protocol advertises it. New step uses notepad on BUBC_windows_sshd_config (operator already has elevated PowerShell so SYSTEM-owned write succeeds), instructs operator to set PasswordAuthentication yes (or add the line if absent), then Restart-Service sshd. Step prose explains this is temporary state — garrison flips back to 'no' as part of its hardening. Placement is post-Start/Enable (sshd_config doesn't exist until first sshd start) and pre-Firewall (so the server is in the correct state when first reachable from network). Step 3 (Start/Enable) gains a one-line note that first start generates the default sshd_config. Reused BUBC_windows_sshd_config (had been retired from handbook in earlier trim, now re-adopted for this step — single source of truth preserved). Validated via tt/buw-hj0.HandbookJurisdictionTop.sh: Windows section now reads 5 numbered steps (Set Password / Install / Start+Enable / Enable PasswordAuth / Firewall) followed by Verify Reachability and Run Garrison sections.

Windows: new Step 1 (Set or Confirm Admin Password) using `net user <admin-user> <temp-password>`, with note that the value stops affecting SSH after garrison hardens sshd_config; existing 3 steps renumber to 2/3/4 via auto-counter.   shared with garrison in `bujb_jurisdiction.sh`. Locked design:
  
  - Two-phase remote-run ceremony forced by the fact that
    `Restart-Service sshd` terminates its own ssh session. Phase 1
    ends with Restart-Service as its final atomic op; phase 2
    reconnects via key auth for any post-state verification. Single
    ssh exec for the restart; bash orchestrator treats the disconnect
    immediately after that exec as the expected phase boundary.
  - `sshd -t` validation is the penultimate atomic op of phase 1
    (immediately before Restart-Service), so a malformed config
    aborts fenestrate before it could brick the running sshd.
  - Posture A — fenestrate writes sshd_config, that is its job.
    Verify-after-write follows the show-raw-then-bash-parse pattern:
    PowerShell `Get-Content` returns raw file bytes; bash-side
    BCG-compliant matching extracts effective directive values. Keeps
    PowerShell out of the parsing business (its error semantics are
    unreliable).
  - Manual password is needed only for the phase-1 initial ssh
    session (handbook precondition: `PasswordAuthentication yes`);
    everything after is automated key auth.
  
  Add BUSJPF subdoc carrying the two-phase operation contract; update
  mapping section + tabtarget catalog (13 → 14).
  
  Narrow `busn_garrison` to workload provisioning only — admin-trust
  front-half moves to fenestrate. Garrison never reads or writes any
  sshd_config; no verification gates. (Fenestrate hardens Windows
  OpenSSH only; for c/w garrisons, Cygwin/WSL sshd hardening is
  operator-manual; for b garrison, Linux/Mac sshd is operator-manual
  likewise.) Garrison's first natural action is admin SSH via key
  auth — failure there surfaces immediately. Update BUSJGB/C/W to
  reflect the workload-only contract. Garrison stays per-shell-letter;
  fenestrate is uniform.
  
  Rename `buw-jpg[bcw]` → `buw-jpG[bcw]` for consistency with
  `buw-jpF`'s capital-letter-for-persistent-config convention (garrison
  is also persistent/destructive; the existing lowercase `g` was
  mis-cased). Spec-only rename — no tabtargets exist on disk yet.
  Discovery recipe for references: `grep -rn "buw-jpg" Tools/buk/`.
  
  Sweep BUS0 drift sites that the dual-purpose pivot left and that
  fenestrate now honestly covers. Discovery recipe (candidates, not
  guaranteed fixes — judge each):
  `grep -rn "out-of-band\|no password fallback\|operator-bootstrapped" Tools/buk/`.
  Reframe §Garrison-Destructive Model paragraph (fenestrate carved off
  admin trust; garrison still subsumes workload lifecycle with no
  separate materialize/remove/enumerate verbs).
  
  Update `buhj_jurisdiction.sh` post-bootstrap hand-off to "Run
  Fenestrate, then Garrison." Linux/Mac stays operator-manual via
  ssh-copy-id handbook line (no fenestrate equivalent). Restructure
  paddock §Current Concept around the two-verb split (the existing
  unified-9-step-ceremony narrative no longer matches either verb
  individually — additive editing won't suffice). Split ceremony
  section into §Fenestrate ceremony + §Garrison ceremony. Adjust
  standing notes that no longer match.
  
  Done when:
  - BUS0 reflects the split with no contradicting prose anywhere
  - BUSJPF + revised BUSJGB/C/W carry their respective contracts
  - Tabtarget catalog has 14 entries with `buw-jpF` added and
    `buw-jpg[bcw]` renamed to `buw-jpG[bcw]` everywhere referenced
  - Handbook and paddock no longer claim garrison handles admin trust
  - `bujb_jurisdiction` body acknowledges housing both verbs
  
  Out of scope: any code (implementation is `implement-jurisdiction`'s
  job); PowerShell garrison + dispatch (deferred per paddock); adopt
  verb (deferred); end-to-end operator test on a Windows node.

ddb9fd9f  ₢A-AAo    Add 'Enable Password Authentication in sshd_config' step to Windows handbook procedure (between Start/Enable Service and Allow Firewall Port). Empirical finding from rocket bootstrap: Windows OpenSSH 9.5 ships with sshd_config in a state that blocks password auth — the wire protocol advertises keyboard-interactive (the standard wrapper for password delivery on Windows) but the server refuses to actually prompt, producing 'Permission denied (publickey,keyboard-interactive)' with no prompt ever shown to the operator. Microsoft Learn confirms: on Windows OpenSSH the only configurable AuthenticationMethods are 'password' and 'publickey' (KbdInteractiveAuthentication is in the not-available list); the server's PasswordAuthentication directive controls both wire methods. Default-shipped state empirically rejects password auth even when the wire protocol advertises it. New step uses notepad on BUBC_windows_sshd_config (operator already has elevated PowerShell so SYSTEM-owned write succeeds), instructs operator to set PasswordAuthentication yes (or add the line if absent), then Restart-Service sshd. Step prose explains this is temporary state — garrison flips back to 'no' as part of its hardening. Placement is post-Start/Enable (sshd_config doesn't exist until first sshd start) and pre-Firewall (so the server is in the correct state when first reachable from network). content.content:
1	# jjezs_paddock ₣A-
2	
3	## Goal
4	
5	Windows test infrastructure for Recipe Bottle. Run RB's container isolation
6	tests on a Windows host with WSL, Cygwin, and PowerShell access paths.
7	
8	## Current Concept
9	
10	**Jurisdiction** is the BUK feature-area for reaching out to remote nodes
11	deterministically. Logical-only umbrella concept; `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` is
12	its implementation seat (BCG-compliant module). Neither the umbrella nor the
13	implementation module earns AXLA-entity voicing in BUS0. Architectural spine
14	in BUS0 §Remote Node Access.
15	
16	**Two regimes (BURN, BURP):**
17	
18	- BURN — node-shape (host, OS family). Per-node, git-transported, changes
19	  rarely. Identifier: viceroyalty.
20	- BURP — privileged + workload credentials. Per-station-user. Four fields:
21	  `BURP_NODE` (viceroyalty reference), `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER`,
22	  `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE`, `BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE`. Pubkeys derived from
23	  privkeys via `ssh-keygen -y` at use-time; no separate pubkey paths or inline
24	  strings. Identifier: investiture.
25	
26	**BURC addition:**
27	
28	- `BURC_WORKLOAD_USER` — project-wide convention name for the workload OS
29	  user that garrison provisions on every node. Single source of truth.
30	
31	**Garrison-destructive model:**
32	
33	- Every garrison wipes prior workload state regardless of which investiture
34	  or operator runs it. Node runtime state belongs to whoever last garrisoned.
35	- One workload user per node, ever. No conscript / discharge / withdraw /
36	  vanquish / inventory verbs; garrison subsumes them.
37	
38	**Cross-cutting premises:**
39	
40	- `burn_host_singularity` — host of a node lives only in BURN; BURP reaches
41	  it by reference, never duplicates.
42	- `bus_keys_operator_owned` — system never generates or modifies SSH key
43	  material; operator owns all key administration.
44	
45	**Tabtarget colophons (13 total; b/c/w shell support, PowerShell deferred):**
46	
47	- `buw-r[np][lrv]` — BURN/BURP regime config (List/Render/Validate) — 6
48	- `buw-jpg[bcw]` — Garrison per shell-letter (b=native bash for Linux/Mac;
49	  c=Cygwin; w=WSL) — 3
50	- `buw-jwk` — Knock (probe workload reachability) — 1
51	- `buw-jwc` — Run command file as workload (shell determined by garrison) — 1
52	- `buw-jws` — Interactive SSH as workload — 1
53	- `buw-hj0` — Handbook jurisdiction top — 1
54	
55	**Operational seat (`bujb_jurisdiction.sh` + `bujb_cli.sh`):**
56	
57	BCG-compliant module hardcoding the three shell-letter→`command=` directive
58	mappings (b/c/w), the workload privkey destination path on remote per
59	shell-letter, the canonical WSL distribution name (`rbtww-main`), and the
60	SSH auth strategy `PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password` (so first-run
61	falls through to ssh's password prompt without garrison-side state
62	detection). SSH transport mechanics live in code; BURP carries no shell
63	selection.
64	
65	**Garrison ceremony (per shell-letter):**
66	
67	1. SSH-as-`BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` with
68	   `PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password`. Steady-state:
69	   `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE` authenticates. First-run: ssh prompts on
70	   /dev/tty; operator types the Windows admin password (Linux/Mac
71	   equivalent); ssh proceeds. Garrison process never sees the password.
72	2. Idempotently place admin pubkey (derived from `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE`
73	   via `ssh-keygen -y`) in admin's authorized_keys with the shell-letter's
74	   `command=` directive.
75	3. Set platform ACL on admin authorized_keys (`icacls` on Windows;
76	   `chmod 600` on Linux/Mac).
77	4. Harden sshd_config: `PubkeyAuthentication yes`,
78	   `PasswordAuthentication no`, `PermitEmptyPasswords no`. Validate config,
79	   restart sshd. Steps 2–4 are the bootstrap front-half — idempotent;
80	   subsequent runs converge as no-ops.
81	5. Destroy existing workload user (if present) via `userdel -r` or platform
82	   equivalent — removes account + home directory; stray files outside home
83	   are workload's own concern, not garrison's.
84	6. Create fresh workload user named `BURC_WORKLOAD_USER` (unprivileged: no
85	   sudo, no admin group, ssh-only access).
86	7. Place workload pubkey in workload's authorized_keys with `command=`.
87	8. Copy workload privkey to remote workload account at the path hardcoded in
88	   `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` per shell-letter (so workload can authenticate
89	   outbound to GitHub).
90	9. Validate round-trip — SSH as workload + exec no-op + verify exit 0.
91	
92	## Heat Sequence
93	
94	**Spec foundation:**
95	- spec-bus0-jurisdiction — BUS0 rewrite reflecting current concept
96	
97	**Implementation:**
98	- implement-jurisdiction — drop BURW infrastructure, four-field BURP,
99	  `BURC_WORKLOAD_USER`, `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` module, 13 tabtargets,
100	  garrison ceremony, cleanup superseded colophons + handbook entries
101	
102	**Investigation (independent, runs anytime):**
103	- AAe — localhost-fundus-parallel-saturation diagnosis
104	
105	**Practice (Windows host, blocks on implementation):**
106	- AAD — WSL distro + Cygwin install
107	- AAE — JJK fundus inside WSL
108	- AAF — Docker dual-daemon
109	
110	## Deferred (named for future return)
111	
112	- **PowerShell garrison + dispatch** — `buw-jpgp` tabtarget + the `p`
113	  shell-letter mapping in `bujb_jurisdiction.sh`. Current heat ships b/c/w
114	  only.
115	- **Adopt** — cross-station-user pubkey install via existing privileged trust.
116	  Today: each station user runs the ceremony fresh from their own machine.
117	- **User regime** — centralized per-user pubkey for shared admin scenarios.
118	- **Separate workload→GitHub keypair** — current shape reuses workload SSH
119	  keypair for outbound; tighten to per-purpose separation if security demands.
120	- **BURP override fields for non-conventional key layouts** — operators with
121	  centralized SSH key storage, non-`.pub`-suffix layouts.
122	- **Localhost garrison/dispatch variants** — deferred; remote nodes only.
123	- **Git normalization on remote** — JJK has `jjx_plant`; BUK doesn't duplicate.
124	
125	## Standing Notes
126	
127	- **Garrison handles first-time admin trust establishment.** On a fresh
128	  node, ssh's `PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password` falls through
129	  to a password prompt on /dev/tty; operator types the Windows admin
130	  password once. Garrison then installs the admin pubkey, sets the platform
131	  ACL, hardens sshd_config (`PasswordAuthentication no`), restarts sshd,
132	  and proceeds with the destructive workload ceremony — all in one
133	  tabtarget. Subsequent runs use key auth automatically. The operator's
134	  manual scope reduces to: install OpenSSH server, enable the service,
135	  allow the firewall port, ensure the admin user has a known password.
136	- **Encrypted (passphrase-protected) privkeys not supported.** `ssh-keygen -y`
137	  prompts interactively; garrison would hang. Operator policy: privkeys
138	  unencrypted; station-level security covers privkey-at-rest protection.
139	- **Workload pubkey must be pre-registered with GitHub out-of-band.** Operator
140	  pastes the pubkey into GitHub (deploy key per repo or personal SSH key)
141	  before garrison runs. Garrison cannot validate this; it is a precondition.
142	- **WSL distribution is hardcoded to `rbtww-main`** in
143	  `bujb_jurisdiction.sh`. If the current default WSL distribution differs,
144	  garrison-WSL fails fast with a clear error.
145	- **Some `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` values may contain spaces** (e.g., Windows
146	  admin user `b hyslop`); audit shell quoting at every variable expansion and
147	  path construction.
148	- Tailscale provides stable transport (Mac sees `rocket` as `100.x.y.z` /
149	  tailnet hostname); not adopted as a BUK dependency.
150	- Fundus capability registry: `Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSFR-FundusRegistry.md`.
151	- PowerShell-from-WSL pattern: bash owns control flow, PowerShell is the
152	  Windows syscall layer; one atomic command per call.
153	- `command=` in authorized_keys is OpenSSH-standard (used by gitolite, GitHub,
154	  rsync, borg); shrinks key blast radius rather than expanding it.
155	
156	# jjezs_pace ₢A-AAp
157	
158	## Character
159	
160	Design landing — multiple spec files, handbook, and paddock must end
161	agreeing on a new verb decomposition. Spec-internal consistency is the
162	load-bearing property; not a mechanical sweep.

181	- Posture A — fenestrate writes sshd_config, that is its job.

# jjezs_paddock ₣A-

## Goal

Windows test infrastructure for Recipe Bottle. Run RB's container isolation
tests on a Windows host with WSL, Cygwin, and PowerShell access paths.

## Current Concept

**Jurisdiction** is the BUK feature-area for reaching out to remote nodes
deterministically. Logical-only umbrella concept; `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` is
its implementation seat (BCG-compliant module). Neither the umbrella nor the
implementation module earns AXLA-entity voicing in BUS0. Architectural spine
in BUS0 §Remote Node Access.

**Two regimes (BURN, BURP):**

- BURN — node-shape (host, OS family). Per-node, git-transported, changes
  rarely. Identifier: viceroyalty.
- BURP — privileged + workload credentials. Per-station-user. Four fields:
  `BURP_NODE` (viceroyalty reference), `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER`,
  `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE`, `BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE`. Pubkeys derived from
  privkeys via `ssh-keygen -y` at use-time; no separate pubkey paths or inline
  strings. Identifier: investiture.

**BURC addition:**

- `BURC_WORKLOAD_USER` — project-wide convention name for the workload OS
  user that garrison provisions on every node. Single source of truth.

**Garrison-destructive model:**

- Every garrison wipes prior workload state regardless of which investiture
  or operator runs it. Node runtime state belongs to whoever last garrisoned.
- One workload user per node, ever. No conscript / discharge / withdraw /
  vanquish / inventory verbs; garrison subsumes them.

**Cross-cutting premises:**

- `burn_host_singularity` — host of a node lives only in BURN; BURP reaches
  it by reference, never duplicates.
- `bus_keys_operator_owned` — system never generates or modifies SSH key
  material; operator owns all key administration.

**Tabtarget colophons (13 total; b/c/w shell support, PowerShell deferred):**

- `buw-r[np][lrv]` — BURN/BURP regime config (List/Render/Validate) — 6
- `buw-jpg[bcw]` — Garrison per shell-letter (b=native bash for Linux/Mac;
  c=Cygwin; w=WSL) — 3
- `buw-jwk` — Knock (probe workload reachability) — 1
- `buw-jwc` — Run command file as workload (shell determined by garrison) — 1
- `buw-jws` — Interactive SSH as workload — 1
- `buw-hj0` — Handbook jurisdiction top — 1

**Operational seat (`bujb_jurisdiction.sh` + `bujb_cli.sh`):**

BCG-compliant module hardcoding the three shell-letter→`command=` directive
mappings (b/c/w), the workload privkey destination path on remote per
shell-letter, the canonical WSL distribution name (`rbtww-main`), and the
SSH auth strategy `PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password` (so first-run
falls through to ssh's password prompt without garrison-side state
detection). SSH transport mechanics live in code; BURP carries no shell
selection.

**Garrison ceremony (per shell-letter):**

1. SSH-as-`BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` with
   `PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password`. Steady-state:
   `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE` authenticates. First-run: ssh prompts on
   /dev/tty; operator types the Windows admin password (Linux/Mac
   equivalent); ssh proceeds. Garrison process never sees the password.
2. Idempotently place admin pubkey (derived from `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE`
   via `ssh-keygen -y`) in admin's authorized_keys with the shell-letter's
   `command=` directive.
3. Set platform ACL on admin authorized_keys (`icacls` on Windows;
   `chmod 600` on Linux/Mac).
4. Harden sshd_config: `PubkeyAuthentication yes`,
   `PasswordAuthentication no`, `PermitEmptyPasswords no`. Validate config,
   restart sshd. Steps 2–4 are the bootstrap front-half — idempotent;
   subsequent runs converge as no-ops.
5. Destroy existing workload user (if present) via `userdel -r` or platform
   equivalent — removes account + home directory; stray files outside home
   are workload's own concern, not garrison's.
6. Create fresh workload user named `BURC_WORKLOAD_USER` (unprivileged: no
   sudo, no admin group, ssh-only access).
7. Place workload pubkey in workload's authorized_keys with `command=`.
8. Copy workload privkey to remote workload account at the path hardcoded in
   `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` per shell-letter (so workload can authenticate
   outbound to GitHub).
9. Validate round-trip — SSH as workload + exec no-op + verify exit 0.

## Heat Sequence

**Spec foundation:**
- spec-bus0-jurisdiction — BUS0 rewrite reflecting current concept

**Implementation:**
- implement-jurisdiction — drop BURW infrastructure, four-field BURP,
  `BURC_WORKLOAD_USER`, `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` module, 13 tabtargets,
  garrison ceremony, cleanup superseded colophons + handbook entries

**Investigation (independent, runs anytime):**
- AAe — localhost-fundus-parallel-saturation diagnosis

**Practice (Windows host, blocks on implementation):**
- AAD — WSL distro + Cygwin install
- AAE — JJK fundus inside WSL
- AAF — Docker dual-daemon

## Deferred (named for future return)

- **PowerShell garrison + dispatch** — `buw-jpgp` tabtarget + the `p`
  shell-letter mapping in `bujb_jurisdiction.sh`. Current heat ships b/c/w
  only.
- **Adopt** — cross-station-user pubkey install via existing privileged trust.
  Today: each station user runs the ceremony fresh from their own machine.
- **User regime** — centralized per-user pubkey for shared admin scenarios.
- **Separate workload→GitHub keypair** — current shape reuses workload SSH
  keypair for outbound; tighten to per-purpose separation if security demands.
- **BURP override fields for non-conventional key layouts** — operators with
  centralized SSH key storage, non-`.pub`-suffix layouts.
- **Localhost garrison/dispatch variants** — deferred; remote nodes only.
- **Git normalization on remote** — JJK has `jjx_plant`; BUK doesn't duplicate.

## Standing Notes

- **Garrison handles first-time admin trust establishment.** On a fresh
  node, ssh's `PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password` falls through
  to a password prompt on /dev/tty; operator types the Windows admin
  password once. Garrison then installs the admin pubkey, sets the platform
  ACL, hardens sshd_config (`PasswordAuthentication no`), restarts sshd,
  and proceeds with the destructive workload ceremony — all in one
  tabtarget. Subsequent runs use key auth automatically. The operator's
  manual scope reduces to: install OpenSSH server, enable the service,
  allow the firewall port, ensure the admin user has a known password.
- **Encrypted (passphrase-protected) privkeys not supported.** `ssh-keygen -y`
  prompts interactively; garrison would hang. Operator policy: privkeys
  unencrypted; station-level security covers privkey-at-rest protection.
- **Workload pubkey must be pre-registered with GitHub out-of-band.** Operator
  pastes the pubkey into GitHub (deploy key per repo or personal SSH key)
  before garrison runs. Garrison cannot validate this; it is a precondition.
- **WSL distribution is hardcoded to `rbtww-main`** in
  `bujb_jurisdiction.sh`. If the current default WSL distribution differs,
  garrison-WSL fails fast with a clear error.
- **Some `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` values may contain spaces** (e.g., Windows
  admin user `b hyslop`); audit shell quoting at every variable expansion and
  path construction.
- Tailscale provides stable transport (Mac sees `rocket` as `100.x.y.z` /
  tailnet hostname); not adopted as a BUK dependency.
- Fundus capability registry: `Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSFR-FundusRegistry.md`.
- PowerShell-from-WSL pattern: bash owns control flow, PowerShell is the
  Windows syscall layer; one atomic command per call.
- `command=` in authorized_keys is OpenSSH-standard (used by gitolite, GitHub,
  rsync, borg); shrinks key blast radius rather than expanding it.

# jjezs_pace ₢A-AAp

## Character

Design landing — multiple spec files, handbook, and paddock must end
agreeing on a new verb decomposition. Spec-internal consistency is the
load-bearing property; not a mechanical sweep.

## Docket

Introduce `busn_fenestrate` as a new jurisdiction verb. Targets
Windows OpenSSH only (hardens its `sshd_config`), uniform (no
shell-letter), tabtarget `buw-jpF` (capital F = persistent config
application; takes BURP investiture as param1), implementation seat
shared with garrison in `bujb_jurisdiction.sh`. Locked design:

- Two-phase remote-run ceremony forced by the fact that
  `Restart-Service sshd` terminates its own ssh session. Phase 1
  ends with Restart-Service as its final atomic op; phase 2
  reconnects via key auth for any post-state verification. Single
  ssh exec for the restart; bash orchestrator treats the disconnect
  immediately after that exec as the expected phase boundary.
- `sshd -t` validation is the penultimate atomic op of phase 1
  (immediately before Restart-Service), so a malformed config
  aborts fenestrate before it could brick the running sshd.
- Posture A — fenestrate writes sshd_config, that is its job.
  Verify-after-write follows the show-raw-then-bash-parse pattern:
  PowerShell `Get-Content` returns raw file bytes; bash-side
  BCG-compliant matching extracts effective directive values. Keeps
  PowerShell out of the parsing business (its error semantics are
  unreliable).
- Manual password is needed only for the phase-1 initial ssh
  session (handbook precondition: `PasswordAuthentication yes`);
  everything after is automated key auth.

Add BUSJPF subdoc carrying the two-phase operation contract; update
mapping section + tabtarget catalog (13 → 14).

Narrow `busn_garrison` to workload provisioning only — admin-trust
front-half moves to fenestrate. Garrison never reads or writes any
sshd_config; no verification gates. (Fenestrate hardens Windows
OpenSSH only; for c/w garrisons, Cygwin/WSL sshd hardening is
operator-manual; for b garrison, Linux/Mac sshd is operator-manual
likewise.) Garrison's first natural action is admin SSH via key
auth — failure there surfaces immediately. Update BUSJGB/C/W to
reflect the workload-only contract. Garrison stays per-shell-letter;
fenestrate is uniform.

Rename `buw-jpg[bcw]` → `buw-jpG[bcw]` for consistency with
`buw-jpF`'s capital-letter-for-persistent-config convention (garrison
is also persistent/destructive; the existing lowercase `g` was
mis-cased). Spec-only rename — no tabtargets exist on disk yet.
Discovery recipe for references: `grep -rn "buw-jpg" Tools/buk/`.

Sweep BUS0 drift sites that the dual-purpose pivot left and that
fenestrate now honestly covers. Discovery recipe (candidates, not
guaranteed fixes — judge each):
`grep -rn "out-of-band\|no password fallback\|operator-bootstrapped" Tools/buk/`.
Reframe §Garrison-Destructive Model paragraph (fenestrate carved off
admin trust; garrison still subsumes workload lifecycle with no
separate materialize/remove/enumerate verbs).

Update `buhj_jurisdiction.sh` post-bootstrap hand-off to "Run
Fenestrate, then Garrison." Linux/Mac stays operator-manual via
ssh-copy-id handbook line (no fenestrate equivalent). Restructure
paddock §Current Concept around the two-verb split (the existing
unified-9-step-ceremony narrative no longer matches either verb
individually — additive editing won't suffice). Split ceremony
section into §Fenestrate ceremony + §Garrison ceremony. Adjust
standing notes that no longer match.

Done when:
- BUS0 reflects the split with no contradicting prose anywhere
- BUSJPF + revised BUSJGB/C/W carry their respective contracts
- Tabtarget catalog has 14 entries with `buw-jpF` added and
  `buw-jpg[bcw]` renamed to `buw-jpG[bcw]` everywhere referenced
- Handbook and paddock no longer claim garrison handles admin trust
- `bujb_jurisdiction` body acknowledges housing both verbs

Out of scope: any code (implementation is `implement-jurisdiction`'s
job); PowerShell garrison + dispatch (deferred per paddock); adopt
verb (deferred); end-to-end operator test on a Windows node.

content.text:
Mounted ₣A- — `rbk-mvp-3-draft-windows-procedures`.

**Heat shape (paddock):** Jurisdiction is BUK's remote-node feature area. Two regimes (BURN node-shape, BURP credentials), garrison-destructive model. Currently 13 tabtargets, ceremony documented as a single 9-step Garrison.

**Pace ₢A-AAp — introduce-fenestrate-narrow-garrison [rough]:** Design landing splitting the unified Garrison ceremony into two verbs:
- **`busn_fenestrate`** (new) — Windows-OpenSSH-only sshd_config harden. Uniform (no shell-letter), tabtarget `buw-jpF`. Two-phase ceremony forced by `Restart-Service sshd` killing its own session.
- **`busn_garrison`** (narrowed) — workload provisioning only. Per-shell-letter (b/c/w). Never touches sshd_config.

**Proposed approach:**
- Discovery sweeps first: `grep -rn "buw-jpg" Tools/buk/` and `grep -rn "out-of-band\|no password fallback\|operator-bootstrapped" Tools/buk/` to ground the edits in actual sites.
- Mint BUSJPF subdoc; update BUS0 mapping section, drift sites, catalog (13→14), reframe §Garrison-Destructive Model paragraph.
- Narrow BUSJGB/C/W to workload-only.
- Update `buhj_jurisdiction.sh` post-bootstrap hand-off.
- Paddock: restructure §Current Concept around two-verb split, split ceremony section, adjust standing notes.

**Silks:** `introduce-fenestrate-narrow-garrison` matches docket — no rename.

**Strategy:** Sequential opus — design landing where spec-internal consistency is the load-bearing property; not parallelizable across files.

Ready to proceed?

47	//axhos_step
48	. *Harden sshd_config*: ensure `PubkeyAuthentication yes`,
49	`PasswordAuthentication no`, `PermitEmptyPasswords no`. Validate
50	(`sshd -t`) and restart sshd. Idempotent — already-hardened configs
51	re-converge to the same state and the restart is a no-op effect on
52	established sessions. After this step the password-fallback path used
53	in step 1 is closed; subsequent runs authenticate by key.
54	
55	//axhos_step
56	. *Destroy workload*: remove any existing {burc_workload_user} account
57	via `userdel -r`.
58	
59	//axhos_step
60	. *Create workload*: provision {burc_workload_user} as an unprivileged,
61	ssh-only account.
62	
63	//axhos_step
64	. *Place workload trust*: write the workload pubkey
65	(derived via `ssh-keygen -y`) to the workload's authorized_keys with
66	the shell-letter `b` `command=` directive.
67	
68	//axhos_step
69	. *Plant workload key*: copy {burp_workload_key_file} to the workload
70	account at the shell-letter `b` destination path hardcoded in
71	{bujb_jurisdiction}.
72	
73	//axhos_step
74	. *Validate round-trip*: SSH as workload, run a no-op command, verify
75	exit 0.
76	
77	//axhog_guarantee
78	On success the node has admin SSH hardened to key-only with the
79	operator's admin pubkey carrying the shell-letter `b` `command=`
80	directive, and exactly one workload account named
81	{burc_workload_user}, ssh-only, with both inbound (from operator) and
82	outbound (to GitHub or equivalent) key trust in place.
83	
84	//axhoc_completion
85	Exit 0 when the round-trip validation succeeds.
86	Non-zero on admin SSH failure, sshd_config validation or restart
87	failure, account-management failure, key-write failure, or workload
88	validation failure.
89

file.content:
//axhob_operation
{bust_garrison_bash}

NOTE: {busn_garrison} ceremony with native bash as the workload shell,
for Linux and macOS nodes. Dual-purpose: the front-half (steps 1-4)
idempotently establishes admin SSH trust and hardens sshd; the
back-half (steps 5-9) destroys any prior workload account named
{burc_workload_user}
and recreates it from a clean slate.

//axhopt_typed_parameter
{burp_investiture}

//axhoq_precondition
A {burp_regime} profile exists at the given {burp_investiture} and its
referenced {burn_regime} profile is reachable.

//axhoq_precondition
sshd is reachable on the node and {burp_privileged_user} can authenticate
either via {burp_privileged_key_file} (steady-state) or via password
(first run only). Garrison opens its admin SSH session with
`PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password`, so on a fresh node
ssh prompts for the admin password on /dev/tty; garrison itself never
sees the password.

//axhoq_precondition
{burp_workload_key_file} is unencrypted and exists on the operator's
station.

//axhos_step
. *Open admin SSH*: connect as {burp_privileged_user} with
`PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password`. Steady-state:
{burp_privileged_key_file} authenticates. First run: ssh prompts on
/dev/tty for the admin password; operator types it once.

//axhos_step
. *Place admin trust*: idempotently install the admin pubkey
(derived from {burp_privileged_key_file} via `ssh-keygen -y`) in
admin's authorized_keys with the shell-letter `b` `command=` directive
from {bujb_jurisdiction}. Re-runs converge as a no-op when the line
is already present.

//axhos_step
. *Lock down admin authorized_keys*: `chmod 600` and `chown` to the
admin user. Idempotent.

//axhos_step
. *Harden sshd_config*: ensure `PubkeyAuthentication yes`,
`PasswordAuthentication no`, `PermitEmptyPasswords no`. Validate
(`sshd -t`) and restart sshd. Idempotent — already-hardened configs
re-converge to the same state and the restart is a no-op effect on
established sessions. After this step the password-fallback path used
in step 1 is closed; subsequent runs authenticate by key.

//axhos_step
. *Destroy workload*: remove any existing {burc_workload_user} account
via `userdel -r`.

//axhos_step
. *Create workload*: provision {burc_workload_user} as an unprivileged,
ssh-only account.

//axhos_step
. *Place workload trust*: write the workload pubkey
(derived via `ssh-keygen -y`) to the workload's authorized_keys with
the shell-letter `b` `command=` directive.

//axhos_step
. *Plant workload key*: copy {burp_workload_key_file} to the workload
account at the shell-letter `b` destination path hardcoded in
{bujb_jurisdiction}.

//axhos_step
. *Validate round-trip*: SSH as workload, run a no-op command, verify
exit 0.

//axhog_guarantee
On success the node has admin SSH hardened to key-only with the
operator's admin pubkey carrying the shell-letter `b` `command=`
directive, and exactly one workload account named
{burc_workload_user}, ssh-only, with both inbound (from operator) and
outbound (to GitHub or equivalent) key trust in place.

//axhoc_completion
Exit 0 when the round-trip validation succeeds.
Non-zero on admin SSH failure, sshd_config validation or restart
failure, account-management failure, key-write failure, or workload
validation failure.

1	//axhob_operation
2	{bust_garrison_cygwin}
3	
4	NOTE: {busn_garrison} ceremony with Cygwin bash as the workload shell,
5	for Windows nodes reached via Cygwin sshd.
6	Destructive: removes any prior workload account named
7	{burc_workload_user}
8	and recreates it from a clean slate.
9	
10	//axhopt_typed_parameter
11	{burp_investiture}
12	
13	//axhoq_precondition
14	A {burp_regime} profile exists at the given {burp_investiture} and its
15	referenced {burn_regime} profile is reachable.
16	
17	//axhoq_precondition
18	Cygwin sshd is installed on the node and {burp_privileged_user}
19	(member of the Administrators group) trusts {burp_privileged_key_file}
20	for key-only SSH (operator-bootstrapped out-of-band).
21	
22	//axhoq_precondition
23	{burp_workload_key_file} is unencrypted and exists on the operator's
24	station.
25	
26	//axhos_step
27	. *Open admin SSH*: connect as {burp_privileged_user} using
28	{burp_privileged_key_file}; key-auth only.
29	
30	//axhos_step
31	. *Place admin trust*: idempotently install the admin pubkey
32	(derived via `ssh-keygen -y`) in admin's authorized_keys with the
33	shell-letter `c` `command=` directive from {bujb_jurisdiction}.
34	
35	//axhos_step
36	. *Destroy workload*: remove any existing {burc_workload_user} Cygwin
37	account via the Cygwin user-management equivalent of `userdel -r`.
38	
39	//axhos_step
40	. *Create workload*: provision {burc_workload_user} as an unprivileged,
41	ssh-only Cygwin account.
42	
43	//axhos_step
44	. *Place workload trust*: write the workload pubkey
45	(derived via `ssh-keygen -y`) to the workload's authorized_keys with
46	the shell-letter `c` `command=` directive.
47	
48	//axhos_step
49	. *Plant workload key*: copy {burp_workload_key_file} to the workload
50	account at the shell-letter `c` destination path hardcoded in
51	{bujb_jurisdiction}.
52	
53	//axhos_step
54	. *Validate round-trip*: SSH as workload, run a no-op command, verify
55	exit 0.
56	
57	//axhog_guarantee
58	On success the node has exactly one workload account named
59	{burc_workload_user} reachable via Cygwin bash.
60	
61	//axhoc_completion
62	Exit 0 when the round-trip validation succeeds.
63	Non-zero on admin SSH failure, Cygwin account-management failure,
64	key-write failure, or workload validation failure.
65

1	//axhob_operation
2	{bust_garrison_wsl}
3	
4	NOTE: {busn_garrison} ceremony with WSL bash as the workload shell,
5	for Windows nodes reached via the WSL distribution `rbtww-main`
6	(hardcoded in {bujb_jurisdiction}).
7	Destructive: removes any prior workload account named
8	{burc_workload_user}
9	and recreates it from a clean slate.
10	
11	//axhopt_typed_parameter
12	{burp_investiture}
13	
14	//axhoq_precondition
15	A {burp_regime} profile exists at the given {burp_investiture} and its
16	referenced {burn_regime} profile is reachable.
17	
18	//axhoq_precondition
19	The default WSL distribution on the node is `rbtww-main` and its sshd
20	is installed; mismatch fails fast with a clear error.
21	
22	//axhoq_precondition
23	{burp_privileged_user} (member of the Administrators group) trusts
24	{burp_privileged_key_file} for key-only SSH against the WSL distribution
25	(operator-bootstrapped out-of-band).
26	
27	//axhoq_precondition
28	{burp_workload_key_file} is unencrypted and exists on the operator's
29	station.
30	
31	//axhos_step
32	. *Open admin SSH*: connect as {burp_privileged_user} using
33	{burp_privileged_key_file}; key-auth only into the WSL distribution.
34	
35	//axhos_step
36	. *Place admin trust*: idempotently install the admin pubkey
37	(derived via `ssh-keygen -y`) in admin's authorized_keys with the
38	shell-letter `w` `command=` directive from {bujb_jurisdiction}.
39	
40	//axhos_step
41	. *Destroy workload*: remove any existing {burc_workload_user} account
42	inside the WSL distribution via `userdel -r`.
43	
44	//axhos_step
45	. *Create workload*: provision {burc_workload_user} as an unprivileged,
46	ssh-only Linux account inside `rbtww-main`.
47	
48	//axhos_step
49	. *Place workload trust*: write the workload pubkey
50	(derived via `ssh-keygen -y`) to the workload's authorized_keys with
51	the shell-letter `w` `command=` directive.
52	
53	//axhos_step
54	. *Plant workload key*: copy {burp_workload_key_file} to the workload
55	account at the shell-letter `w` destination path hardcoded in
56	{bujb_jurisdiction}.
57	
58	//axhos_step
59	. *Validate round-trip*: SSH as workload, run a no-op command, verify
60	exit 0.
61	
62	//axhog_guarantee
63	On success the node has exactly one workload account named
64	{burc_workload_user} reachable via the WSL distribution `rbtww-main`.
65	
66	//axhoc_completion
67	Exit 0 when the round-trip validation succeeds.
68	Non-zero on WSL distribution mismatch, admin SSH failure, account-
69	management failure, key-write failure, or workload validation failure.
70

content.content:
1	= Bash Utilities Specification
2	:doctype: book
3	
4	// tag::mapping-section[]
5	//
6	// Category declarations:
7	// bust_:  Tabtarget (launcher naming vesture)
8	// busd:   Dispatch (non-terminal, tabtarget system umbrella)
9	// busdc_: Dispatch Colophon (routing identifier)
10	// busdf_: Dispatch Frontispiece (human-readable description)
11	// busdi_: Dispatch Imprint (embedded parameter/target specifier)
12	// busdm_: Dispatch Formulary (routing component)
13	// busdl_: Dispatch Launcher (bootstrap script)
14	// bukro_: Kit Regime Operation (kindle/validate/render)
15	// burc_:  Configuration Regime (project-level settings)
16	// burn_:  Node Regime (per-node SSH connection profiles, multi-instance)
17	// burs_:  Station Regime (developer-level settings)
18	// burd_:  Dispatch Runtime (ephemeral state assembled during dispatch)
19	// bure_:  Environment Regime (ambient caller-supplied behavioral overrides)
20	// busi:   Invocation (test invocation umbrella)
21	// busn_:  Node Access (verbs for remote node management)
22	// busit_: Invocation Tier (tier classification)
23	// busig_: Invocation Global (capture state set by zbuto_invoke)
24	// busx_:  Case Boundary (test case isolation runner)
25	// busff_: Fact-File (inter-step communication via output files)
26	// buf_:   Fact Module (dual-write fact-file primitives)
27	// busy_:  Yelp (pre-rendered formatted string capture)
28	// burx_:  Exchange Regime (cross-project dispatch state)
29	//
30	// Variant patterns:
31	//   _s: plural form
32	//   _p: possessive form
33	//
34	
35	// Tabtarget Vesture
36	:bust_tabtarget:        <<bust_tabtarget,Tabtarget Vesture>>
37	
38	// Jurisdiction Tabtargets
39	:bust_garrison_bash:               <<bust_garrison_bash,Garrison Bash>>
40	:bust_garrison_cygwin:             <<bust_garrison_cygwin,Garrison Cygwin>>

= Bash Utilities Specification
:doctype: book

// tag::mapping-section[]
//
// Category declarations:
// bust_:  Tabtarget (launcher naming vesture)
// busd:   Dispatch (non-terminal, tabtarget system umbrella)
// busdc_: Dispatch Colophon (routing identifier)
// busdf_: Dispatch Frontispiece (human-readable description)
// busdi_: Dispatch Imprint (embedded parameter/target specifier)
// busdm_: Dispatch Formulary (routing component)
// busdl_: Dispatch Launcher (bootstrap script)
// bukro_: Kit Regime Operation (kindle/validate/render)
// burc_:  Configuration Regime (project-level settings)
// burn_:  Node Regime (per-node SSH connection profiles, multi-instance)
// burs_:  Station Regime (developer-level settings)
// burd_:  Dispatch Runtime (ephemeral state assembled during dispatch)
// bure_:  Environment Regime (ambient caller-supplied behavioral overrides)
// busi:   Invocation (test invocation umbrella)
// busn_:  Node Access (verbs for remote node management)
// busit_: Invocation Tier (tier classification)
// busig_: Invocation Global (capture state set by zbuto_invoke)
// busx_:  Case Boundary (test case isolation runner)
// busff_: Fact-File (inter-step communication via output files)
// buf_:   Fact Module (dual-write fact-file primitives)
// busy_:  Yelp (pre-rendered formatted string capture)
// burx_:  Exchange Regime (cross-project dispatch state)
//
// Variant patterns:
//   _s: plural form
//   _p: possessive form
//

Resolves to the
{burn_regime}
file at the directory bearing this name.

[[burp_privileged_user]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_string
{burp_privileged_user}::
Remote admin OS user this credential authenticates as for the
privileged garrison ceremony
(e.g., `Administrator` on Windows, `root` or a sudo-capable user on
Linux/Mac).
Garrison runs against this account to provision the workload account.
+
Some platforms permit privileged usernames containing spaces
(e.g., `b hyslop`); the implementation must quote correctly at every
expansion.

[[burp_privileged_key_file]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path
{burp_privileged_key_file}::
Path to the operator-managed SSH private key authorized as
{burp_privileged_user}
on the node per
{bus_keys_operator_owned}.
The matching public key is derived at use-time via `ssh-keygen -y`;
no separate pubkey path is stored.
+
Garrison authenticates using this key only — there is no password fallback.
First-time admin trust is established out-of-band via the bootstrap
handbook before garrison runs.

[[burp_workload_key_file]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path
{burp_workload_key_file}::
Path to the operator-managed SSH private key the workload account uses
to authenticate inbound (from the operator's station) and outbound
(to GitHub or other services).
Garrison copies this private key onto the remote workload account at a
shell-letter-specific path hardcoded in
{bujb_jurisdiction};
the matching public key is derived via `ssh-keygen -y` for
authorized_keys placement on both sides of the trip.
+
The workload pubkey must be pre-registered with any outbound service
(e.g., GitHub deploy keys) out-of-band; garrison cannot validate that
registration.

=== BURD Regime

[[burd_prefix]]
//axl_voices axrg_prefix

Bash Utility Dispatch Runtime

[[burd_regime]]
//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_constructed axrd_singleton
{burd_regime}::
Ephemeral runtime state assembled by bud_dispatch.sh during tabtarget execution.
Unlike file-sourced regimes, BURD variables are constructed inline — no assignment file exists.
The dispatch main function IS the kindle: zbud_setup sources configuration and derives computed state, zbud_process_args parses the tabtarget into tokens.

content.content:
1456	== Remote Node Access
1457	
1458	BUK reaches remote nodes under the
1459	{bus_jurisdiction}
1460	umbrella: a single station user provisions one workload account per
1461	node and dispatches commands to it. Privileged trust is the operator's
1462	prerequisite, not BUK's deliverable — first-time admin SSH bootstrap
1463	is an out-of-band handbook procedure.
1464	
1465	[[bus_jurisdiction]]
1466	{bus_jurisdiction}::
1467	The BUK feature-area for reaching remote nodes deterministically:
1468	operator-managed admin trust as input,
1469	{busn_garrison}
1470	to provision and refresh a workload account, and the workload-side
1471	tabtargets ({bust_knock}, {bust_command_file}, {bust_interactive_session})
1472	to dispatch work.
1473	+
1474	{bus_jurisdiction}
1475	is logical only — there is no AXLA-typed entity behind the umbrella.
1476	Its implementation seat is
1477	{bujb_jurisdiction}.
1478	
1479	[[bujb_jurisdiction]]
1480	{bujb_jurisdiction}::
1481	The BCG-compliant module
1482	(`Tools/buk/bujb_jurisdiction.sh` plus its `bujb_cli.sh` companion)
1483	that hardcodes the
1484	{bus_jurisdiction}
1485	contracts not expressed in regime data:
1486	the three shell-letter→`command=` directive mappings
1487	(`b` = native bash for Linux/Mac;
1488	`c` = Cygwin;
1489	`w` = WSL),
1490	the workload private-key destination paths on the remote per shell-letter,
1491	and the canonical WSL distribution name (`rbtww-main`).
1492	+
1493	SSH transport mechanics live in code;
1494	{burp_regime}
1495	carries no shell selection.
1496	
1497	=== Privileges
1498	
1499	A
1500	{burn_regime}
1501	instance lives under `.buk/rbmn_nodes/<{burn_viceroyalty}>/` and is shared
1502	across station users (git-transported); it carries node shape only and
1503	no key material.
1504	A
1505	{burp_regime}
1506	instance lives under `.buk/rbmu_users/<{burs_user}>/<{burp_investiture}>/`;
1507	the
1508	{burs_user}
1509	owns its credential subtree exclusively, and operations write only
1510	under the current station user's tree.
1511	
1512	Garrison authenticates as
1513	{burp_privileged_user}
1514	using
1515	{burp_privileged_key_file}
1516	(key auth only — no password fallback).
1517	Workload account is unprivileged, ssh-only, and named by
1518	{burc_workload_user}
1519	(project-wide convention, single source of truth).
1520	
1521	=== Garrison-Destructive Model
1522	
1523	Every garrison invocation wipes prior workload state on the node
1524	regardless of which
1525	{burp_investiture}
1526	or station user runs it.
1527	There is exactly one workload user per node, ever; node runtime state
1528	belongs to whoever last garrisoned it.
1529	Garrison subsumes lifecycle: there is no separate verb to materialize,
1530	remove, or enumerate workload accounts — every garrison rebuilds from a
1531	clean slate.
1532	
1533	=== Tabtarget Catalog
1534	
1535	Thirteen tabtargets cover regime configuration, garrison ceremony, and
1536	workload dispatch.
1537	PowerShell garrison and dispatch are deferred and not enumerated below.
1538	
1539	==== Regime Configuration
1540	
1541	[[bust_node_regime_list]]
1542	//axvo_tabtarget
1543	{bust_node_regime_list}::
1544	`buw-rnl` —
1545	List all {burn_regime} profiles under `.buk/rbmn_nodes/`.
1546	
1547	[[bust_node_regime_render]]
1548	//axvo_tabtarget
1549	{bust_node_regime_render}::
1550	`buw-rnr` —
1551	Render a single {burn_regime} profile
1552	(viceroyalty passed as positional argument).
1553	
1554	[[bust_node_regime_validate]]
1555	//axvo_tabtarget
1556	{bust_node_regime_validate}::
1557	`buw-rnv` —
1558	Validate a single {burn_regime} profile
1559	(viceroyalty passed as positional argument).
1560	
1561	[[bust_privilege_regime_list]]
1562	//axvo_tabtarget
1563	{bust_privilege_regime_list}::
1564	`buw-rpl` —
1565	List all {burp_regime} profiles for the current {burs_user}.
1566	
1567	[[bust_privilege_regime_render]]
1568	//axvo_tabtarget
1569	{bust_privilege_regime_render}::
1570	`buw-rpr` —
1571	Render a single {burp_regime} profile
1572	(investiture passed as positional argument).
1573	
1574	[[bust_privilege_regime_validate]]
1575	//axvo_tabtarget
1576	{bust_privilege_regime_validate}::
1577	`buw-rpv` —
1578	Validate a single {burp_regime} profile
1579	(investiture passed as positional argument).
1580	
1581	==== Garrison (per shell-letter)
1582	
1583	[[bust_garrison_bash]]
1584	//axvo_tabtarget
1585	{bust_garrison_bash}::
1586	`buw-jpgb` —
1587	{busn_garrison} ceremony with native bash as the workload shell
1588	(Linux and macOS nodes).
1589	
1590	[[bust_garrison_cygwin]]
1591	//axvo_tabtarget
1592	{bust_garrison_cygwin}::
1593	`buw-jpgc` —
1594	{busn_garrison} ceremony with Cygwin bash as the workload shell
1595	(Windows nodes via Cygwin sshd).
1596	
1597	[[bust_garrison_wsl]]
1598	//axvo_tabtarget
1599	{bust_garrison_wsl}::
1600	`buw-jpgw` —
1601	{busn_garrison} ceremony with WSL bash as the workload shell
1602	(Windows nodes via WSL distribution `rbtww-main`).
1603	
1604	==== Workload Dispatch
1605	
1606	[[bust_knock]]
1607	//axvo_tabtarget
1608	{bust_knock}::
1609	`buw-jwk` —
1610	Probe workload reachability via key-only SSH;
1611	returns 0 if the workload account answers a no-op command.
1612	
1613	[[bust_command_file]]
1614	//axvo_tabtarget
1615	{bust_command_file}::
1616	`buw-jwc` —
1617	Run the contents of a local command file as the workload account;
1618	shell is determined by whichever shell-letter garrisoned the node.
1619	Captures stdout, stderr, and remote exit status to {burd_output_dir}.
1620	
1621	[[bust_interactive_session]]
1622	//axvo_tabtarget
1623	{bust_interactive_session}::
1624	`buw-jws` —
1625	Open an interactive SSH session to the workload account.
1626	
1627	==== Handbook
1628	
1629	[[bust_handbook_jurisdiction_top]]
1630	//axvo_tabtarget
1631	{bust_handbook_jurisdiction_top}::
1632	`buw-hj0` —
1633	Top-level handbook landing for jurisdiction procedures
1634	(garrison preparation, bootstrap pointers, dispatch usage).
1635	
1636	=== Verb Definitions
1637	
1638	[[busn_garrison]]
1639	//axl_voices axt_command
1640	{busn_garrison}::
1641	Establish or refresh workload trust on a node from an existing
1642	admin SSH foothold.
1643	A single tabtarget per shell-letter
1644	(`buw-jpgb`, `buw-jpgc`, `buw-jpgw`)
1645	runs the same destructive ceremony with the shell-letter selecting
1646	remote shell mappings via
1647	{bujb_jurisdiction}.
1648	Idempotent on re-run; destructive of any prior workload state.
1649	+
1650	Ceremony steps:
1651	+
1652	. SSH as
1653	{burp_privileged_user}
1654	using
1655	{burp_privileged_key_file};
1656	key-auth only.
1657	. Idempotently place admin pubkey
1658	(derived from privkey via `ssh-keygen -y`)
1659	in admin's authorized_keys with the shell-letter's `command=` directive.
1660	. Destroy any existing workload account named
1661	{burc_workload_user}
1662	via `userdel -r` or platform equivalent — removes account and home
1663	directory.
1664	. Create fresh workload account named
1665	{burc_workload_user}:
1666	unprivileged, no sudo, no admin group, ssh-only.
1667	. Place workload pubkey
1668	(derived from
1669	{burp_workload_key_file}
1670	via `ssh-keygen -y`)
1671	in workload's authorized_keys with the shell-letter's `command=`
1672	directive.
1673	. Copy
1674	{burp_workload_key_file}
1675	to the remote workload account at the path hardcoded in
1676	{bujb_jurisdiction}
1677	per shell-letter,
1678	so the workload can authenticate outbound (e.g., to GitHub).
1679	. Validate round-trip: SSH as workload, exec a no-op, verify exit 0.
1680	
1681	=== Input Artifacts
1682	
1683	[[busi_scribed_command_file]]
1684	//axl_voices axo_entity
1685	{busi_scribed_command_file}::
1686	A file authored by the operator containing commands to be executed
1687	on the remote node by {bust_command_file}.
1688	Path passed as a positional argument; contents are streamed
1689	to the remote shell at dispatch time.
1690	
1691	include::BUSTLL-NodeRegimeList.adoc[]
1692	
1693	include::BUSTLR-NodeRegimeRender.adoc[]
1694	
1695	include::BUSTLV-NodeRegimeValidate.adoc[]
1696	
1697	include::BUSTPL-PrivilegeRegimeList.adoc[]
1698	
1699	include::BUSTPR-PrivilegeRegimeRender.adoc[]
1700	
1701	include::BUSTPV-PrivilegeRegimeValidate.adoc[]
1702	
1703	include::BUSJGB-GarrisonBash.adoc[]
1704	
1705	include::BUSJGC-GarrisonCygwin.adoc[]
1706	
1707	include::BUSJGW-GarrisonWsl.adoc[]
1708	
1709	include::BUSJWK-Knock.adoc[]
1710	
1711	include::BUSJWC-CommandFile.adoc[]
1712	
1713	include::BUSJWS-InteractiveSession.adoc[]
1714	
1715	include::BUSJH0-HandbookJurisdictionTop.adoc[]

file.content:
== Remote Node Access

BUK reaches remote nodes under the
{bus_jurisdiction}
umbrella: a single station user provisions one workload account per
node and dispatches commands to it. Privileged trust is the operator's
prerequisite, not BUK's deliverable — first-time admin SSH bootstrap
is an out-of-band handbook procedure.

[[bus_jurisdiction]]
{bus_jurisdiction}::
The BUK feature-area for reaching remote nodes deterministically:
operator-managed admin trust as input,
{busn_garrison}
to provision and refresh a workload account, and the workload-side
tabtargets ({bust_knock}, {bust_command_file}, {bust_interactive_session})
to dispatch work.
+
{bus_jurisdiction}
is logical only — there is no AXLA-typed entity behind the umbrella.
Its implementation seat is
{bujb_jurisdiction}.

[[bujb_jurisdiction]]
{bujb_jurisdiction}::
The BCG-compliant module
(`Tools/buk/bujb_jurisdiction.sh` plus its `bujb_cli.sh` companion)
that hardcodes the
{bus_jurisdiction}
contracts not expressed in regime data:
the three shell-letter→`command=` directive mappings
(`b` = native bash for Linux/Mac;
`c` = Cygwin;
`w` = WSL),
the workload private-key destination paths on the remote per shell-letter,
and the canonical WSL distribution name (`rbtww-main`).
+
SSH transport mechanics live in code;
{burp_regime}
carries no shell selection.

=== Privileges

A
{burn_regime}
instance lives under `.buk/rbmn_nodes/<{burn_viceroyalty}>/` and is shared
across station users (git-transported); it carries node shape only and
no key material.
A
{burp_regime}
instance lives under `.buk/rbmu_users/<{burs_user}>/<{burp_investiture}>/`;
the
{burs_user}
owns its credential subtree exclusively, and operations write only
under the current station user's tree.

Garrison authenticates as
{burp_privileged_user}
using
{burp_privileged_key_file}
(key auth only — no password fallback).
Workload account is unprivileged, ssh-only, and named by
{burc_workload_user}
(project-wide convention, single source of truth).

=== Garrison-Destructive Model

Every garrison invocation wipes prior workload state on the node
regardless of which
{burp_investiture}
or station user runs it.
There is exactly one workload user per node, ever; node runtime state
belongs to whoever last garrisoned it.
Garrison subsumes lifecycle: there is no separate verb to materialize,
remove, or enumerate workload accounts — every garrison rebuilds from a
clean slate.

=== Tabtarget Catalog

Thirteen tabtargets cover regime configuration, garrison ceremony, and
workload dispatch.
PowerShell garrison and dispatch are deferred and not enumerated below.

==== Regime Configuration

[[bust_node_regime_list]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_node_regime_list}::
`buw-rnl` —
List all {burn_regime} profiles under `.buk/rbmn_nodes/`.

[[bust_node_regime_render]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_node_regime_render}::
`buw-rnr` —
Render a single {burn_regime} profile
(viceroyalty passed as positional argument).

[[bust_node_regime_validate]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_node_regime_validate}::
`buw-rnv` —
Validate a single {burn_regime} profile
(viceroyalty passed as positional argument).

[[bust_privilege_regime_list]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_privilege_regime_list}::
`buw-rpl` —
List all {burp_regime} profiles for the current {burs_user}.

[[bust_privilege_regime_render]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_privilege_regime_render}::
`buw-rpr` —
Render a single {burp_regime} profile
(investiture passed as positional argument).

[[bust_privilege_regime_validate]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_privilege_regime_validate}::
`buw-rpv` —
Validate a single {burp_regime} profile
(investiture passed as positional argument).

==== Garrison (per shell-letter)

[[bust_garrison_bash]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_garrison_bash}::
`buw-jpgb` —
{busn_garrison} ceremony with native bash as the workload shell
(Linux and macOS nodes).

[[bust_garrison_cygwin]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_garrison_cygwin}::
`buw-jpgc` —
{busn_garrison} ceremony with Cygwin bash as the workload shell
(Windows nodes via Cygwin sshd).

[[bust_garrison_wsl]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_garrison_wsl}::
`buw-jpgw` —
{busn_garrison} ceremony with WSL bash as the workload shell
(Windows nodes via WSL distribution `rbtww-main`).

==== Workload Dispatch

[[bust_knock]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_knock}::
`buw-jwk` —
Probe workload reachability via key-only SSH;
returns 0 if the workload account answers a no-op command.

[[bust_command_file]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_command_file}::
`buw-jwc` —
Run the contents of a local command file as the workload account;
shell is determined by whichever shell-letter garrisoned the node.
Captures stdout, stderr, and remote exit status to {burd_output_dir}.

[[bust_interactive_session]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_interactive_session}::
`buw-jws` —
Open an interactive SSH session to the workload account.

==== Handbook

[[bust_handbook_jurisdiction_top]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_handbook_jurisdiction_top}::
`buw-hj0` —
Top-level handbook landing for jurisdiction procedures
(garrison preparation, bootstrap pointers, dispatch usage).

=== Verb Definitions

[[busn_garrison]]
//axl_voices axt_command
{busn_garrison}::
Establish or refresh workload trust on a node from an existing
admin SSH foothold.
A single tabtarget per shell-letter
(`buw-jpgb`, `buw-jpgc`, `buw-jpgw`)
runs the same destructive ceremony with the shell-letter selecting
remote shell mappings via
{bujb_jurisdiction}.
Idempotent on re-run; destructive of any prior workload state.
+
Ceremony steps:
+
. SSH as
{burp_privileged_user}
using
{burp_privileged_key_file};
key-auth only.
. Idempotently place admin pubkey
(derived from privkey via `ssh-keygen -y`)
in admin's authorized_keys with the shell-letter's `command=` directive.
. Destroy any existing workload account named
{burc_workload_user}
via `userdel -r` or platform equivalent — removes account and home
directory.
. Create fresh workload account named
{burc_workload_user}:
unprivileged, no sudo, no admin group, ssh-only.
. Place workload pubkey
(derived from
{burp_workload_key_file}
via `ssh-keygen -y`)
in workload's authorized_keys with the shell-letter's `command=`
directive.
. Copy
{burp_workload_key_file}
to the remote workload account at the path hardcoded in
{bujb_jurisdiction}
per shell-letter,
so the workload can authenticate outbound (e.g., to GitHub).
. Validate round-trip: SSH as workload, exec a no-op, verify exit 0.

=== Input Artifacts

[[busi_scribed_command_file]]
//axl_voices axo_entity
{busi_scribed_command_file}::
A file authored by the operator containing commands to be executed
on the remote node by {bust_command_file}.
Path passed as a positional argument; contents are streamed
to the remote shell at dispatch time.

include::BUSTLL-NodeRegimeList.adoc[]

include::BUSTLR-NodeRegimeRender.adoc[]

include::BUSTLV-NodeRegimeValidate.adoc[]

include::BUSTPL-PrivilegeRegimeList.adoc[]

include::BUSTPR-PrivilegeRegimeRender.adoc[]

include::BUSTPV-PrivilegeRegimeValidate.adoc[]

include::BUSJGB-GarrisonBash.adoc[]

include::BUSJGC-GarrisonCygwin.adoc[]

include::BUSJGW-GarrisonWsl.adoc[]

include::BUSJWK-Knock.adoc[]

include::BUSJWC-CommandFile.adoc[]

include::BUSJWS-InteractiveSession.adoc[]

include::BUSJH0-HandbookJurisdictionTop.adoc[]

2	#
3	# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");

7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
8	#

10	#
11	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# Bash Utility Handbook - Jurisdiction Procedures
20	#
21	# Handbook content for BUK's remote-node feature area (BUS0 §Remote
22	# Node Access). Garrison handles first-run admin trust establishment
23	# itself (password-fallback on /dev/tty, then key-only thereafter);
24	# the operator's manual scope is just making sshd reachable on the
25	# network with a known admin password.
26	
27	set -euo pipefail
28	
29	test -z "${ZBUHJ_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module buhj multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
30	ZBUHJ_SOURCED=1
31	
32	######################################################################
33	# Internal: Kindle and Sentinel
34	
35	zbuhj_kindle() {
36	  test -z "${ZBUHJ_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module buhj already kindled"
37	
38	  test -n "${BUBC_windows_ssh_port:-}" || buc_die "buhj requires bubc_constants.sh sourced before kindle"
39	
40	  readonly ZBUHJ_KINDLED=1
41	}
42	
43	zbuhj_sentinel() {
44	  test "${ZBUHJ_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module buhj not kindled - call zbuhj_kindle first"
45	}
46	
47	######################################################################
48	# Internal: Section renderers
49	
50	zbuhj_render_landing() {
51	  buh_section  "Jurisdiction Handbook"
52	  buh_line     "BUK reaches remote nodes through Garrison: a destructive ceremony"
53	  buh_line     "that provisions one workload account per node, dispatched as the"
54	  buh_line     "current station user. Garrison never generates or modifies SSH"
55	  buh_line     "key material; the operator owns all key administration."
56	  buh_e
57	  buh_line     "Garrison handles first-run admin trust itself. Its admin SSH"
58	  buh_line     "session uses PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password — on a"
59	  buh_line     "fresh node ssh falls through to a password prompt on /dev/tty,"
60	  buh_line     "the operator types the admin password once, garrison installs"
61	  buh_line     "the admin pubkey + sshd hardening, and subsequent runs use key"
62	  buh_line     "auth automatically. The operator's manual scope reduces to:"
63	  buh_line     "make sshd reachable on the network with a known admin password."
64	}
65	
66	zbuhj_render_linux_mac_note() {
67	  buh_section  "Linux and macOS"
68	  buh_line     "sshd is typically already installed and reachable. The operator's"
69	  buh_line     "manual scope is just: confirm sshd is running and the host is"
70	  buh_line     "reachable from the station. Garrison handles admin pubkey"
71	  buh_line     "placement and sshd hardening on first run via the password-"
72	  buh_line     "fallback path."
73	  buh_e
74	  buh_line     "If you are unsure of the admin user's password (or it has none),"
75	  buh_line     "set it to a known value first; garrison clears the password-"
76	  buh_line     "fallback path on first run, so the value stops affecting SSH:"
77	  buh_e
78	  buh_code     "sudo passwd <admin-user>"
79	  buh_e
80	  buh_line     "Operators who prefer to pre-establish key trust manually may use"
81	  buh_line     "the standard recipe instead; garrison's first run then converges"
82	  buh_line     "as a no-op on the admin pubkey:"
83	  buh_e
84	  buh_code     "ssh-copy-id -i ~/.ssh/<admin-pubkey>.pub <admin-user>@<host>"
85	}
86	
87	zbuhj_render_windows_bootstrap() {
88	  buh_section  "Windows: sshd Reachability"
89	  buh_line     "All steps run on the Windows host in an elevated PowerShell."
90	  buh_line     "Right-click Start, Terminal (Admin), or search 'PowerShell' and"
91	  buh_line     "Run as Administrator."
92	  buh_e
93	  buh_section  "Preconditions:"
94	  buh_line     "- Windows host with administrator access"
95	  buh_line     "- Network reachable on TCP/${BUBC_windows_ssh_port} from operator's station"
96	  buh_e
97	
98	  buh_step1    "Set or Confirm Admin Password:"
99	  buh_line     "Garrison's first run authenticates as the admin user via password"
100	  buh_line     "(once) before installing the pubkey. If you already know your local"
101	  buh_line     "admin password, skip. Otherwise set it to a known value:"
102	  buh_code     "net user <admin-user> <temp-password>"
103	  buh_line     "After garrison runs, sshd is hardened to key-only and this value"
104	  buh_line     "stops affecting SSH (it remains your Windows logon password — leave"
105	  buh_line     "it or reset to taste)."
106	  buh_e
107	
108	  buh_step1    "Install OpenSSH Server:"
109	  buh_line     "Downloads from Windows Update; may take 10+ minutes."
110	  buh_code     "Add-WindowsCapability -Online -Name OpenSSH.Server~~~~0.0.1.0"
111	  buh_e
112	
113	  buh_step1    "Start and Enable the Service:"
114	  buh_line     "First start also generates the default sshd_config."
115	  buh_code     "Start-Service sshd"
116	  buh_code     "Set-Service -Name sshd -StartupType Automatic"
117	  buh_e
118	
119	  buyy_cmd_yawp "${BUBC_windows_sshd_config}"; local -r z_sshd_config_yelp="${z_buym_yelp}"
120	  buh_step1    "Enable Password Authentication in sshd_config:"
121	  buh_line     "Windows OpenSSH ships with sshd_config in a state that blocks"
122	  buh_line     "garrison's first-run password fallback (the wire protocol"
123	  buh_line     "advertises keyboard-interactive but the server refuses to"
124	  buh_line     "actually prompt). Open sshd_config, find the"
125	  buh_line     "PasswordAuthentication directive (commented or otherwise),"
126	  buh_line     "and set it to exactly:"
127	  buh_e
128	  buh_code     "PasswordAuthentication yes"
129	  buh_e
130	  buh_line     "If no PasswordAuthentication line exists, add one. Save"
131	  buh_line     "(notepad inherits your elevated context so SYSTEM-owned writes"
132	  buh_line     "succeed). Garrison flips this back to 'no' as part of its"
133	  buh_line     "hardening step, so this is a temporary state."
134	  buh_line     "File: ${z_sshd_config_yelp}"
135	  buh_e
136	  buh_code     "notepad ${BUBC_windows_sshd_config}"
137	  buh_code     "Restart-Service sshd"
138	  buh_e
139	
140	  buh_step1    "Allow Port ${BUBC_windows_ssh_port} Through Firewall:"
141	  buh_code     "New-NetFirewallRule -Name ${BUBC_windows_fw_rule_name} -DisplayName \"${BUBC_windows_fw_display_name}\" -Enabled True -Direction Inbound -Protocol TCP -Action Allow -LocalPort ${BUBC_windows_ssh_port}"
142	  buh_e
143	
144	  buh_section  "Verify Reachability"
145	  buh_line     "From the operator's station, confirm sshd answers (a password"
146	  buh_line     "prompt is expected and correct — garrison's first run will"
147	  buh_line     "type it through to ssh on /dev/tty):"
148	  buh_e
149	  buh_code     "ssh <admin-user>@<windows-host>"
150	}
151	
152	zbuhj_render_post_bootstrap() {
153	  buh_section  "Run Garrison"
154	  buh_line     "With sshd reachable, run garrison for the chosen workload shell."
155	  buh_line     "Garrison establishes admin trust on its first run (typing the"
156	  buh_line     "admin password once when ssh prompts on /dev/tty), then destroys"
157	  buh_line     "any prior workload account and provisions a fresh one named"
158	  buh_line     "BURC_WORKLOAD_USER. Subsequent runs use key auth automatically."
159	  buh_e
160	  buh_line     "  buw-jpgb <investiture>  — native bash workload (Linux, macOS)"
161	  buh_line     "  buw-jpgc <investiture>  — Cygwin bash workload (Windows)"
162	  buh_line     "  buw-jpgw <investiture>  — WSL bash workload (Windows)"
163	  buh_e
164	  buh_line     "Choose c or w for Windows by which workload runtime you need."
165	  buh_line     "On Windows, both share this same admin SSH bootstrap; the"
166	  buh_line     "shell-letter only routes garrison's own admin session."
167	  buh_e
168	  buh_line     "After garrison succeeds, dispatch work via the workload"
169	  buh_line     "tabtargets:"
170	  buh_line     "  buw-jwk <investiture>           — probe workload reachability"
171	  buh_line     "  buw-jwc <investiture> <file>    — run a command file"
172	  buh_line     "  buw-jws <investiture>           — interactive workload session"
173	}
174	
175	######################################################################
176	# External Functions (buhj_*)
177	
178	buhj_top() {
179	  zbuhj_sentinel
180	
181	  buc_doc_brief "Display jurisdiction handbook landing + sshd-reachability procedures"
182	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
183	
184	  zbuhj_render_landing
185	  buh_e
186	  zbuhj_render_linux_mac_note
187	  buh_e
188	  zbuhj_render_windows_bootstrap
189	  buh_e
190	  zbuhj_render_post_bootstrap
191	}
192	
193	# eof

#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.

# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
