# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Bash Utility Handbook - Jurisdiction Procedures
#
# Handbook content for BUK's remote-node feature area (BUS0 §Remote
# Node Access). Garrison handles first-run admin trust establishment
# itself (password-fallback on /dev/tty, then key-only thereafter);
# the operator's manual scope is just making sshd reachable on the
# network with a known admin password.

set -euo pipefail

test -z "${ZBUHJ_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module buhj multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZBUHJ_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Internal: Kindle and Sentinel

zbuhj_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZBUHJ_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module buhj already kindled"

  test -n "${BUBC_windows_ssh_port:-}" || buc_die "buhj requires bubc_constants.sh sourced before kindle"

  readonly ZBUHJ_KINDLED=1
}

zbuhj_sentinel() {
  test "${ZBUHJ_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module buhj not kindled - call zbuhj_kindle first"
}

######################################################################
# Internal: Section renderers

zbuhj_render_landing() {
  buh_section  "Jurisdiction Handbook"
  buh_line     "BUK reaches remote nodes through Garrison: a destructive ceremony"
  buh_line     "that provisions one workload account per node, dispatched as the"
  buh_line     "current station user. Garrison never generates or modifies SSH"
  buh_line     "key material; the operator owns all key administration."
  buh_e
  buh_line     "Garrison handles first-run admin trust itself. Its admin SSH"
  buh_line     "session uses PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password — on a"
  buh_line     "fresh node ssh falls through to a password prompt on /dev/tty,"
  buh_line     "the operator types the admin password once, garrison installs"
  buh_line     "the admin pubkey + sshd hardening, and subsequent runs use key"
  buh_line     "auth automatically. The operator's manual scope reduces to:"
  buh_line     "make sshd reachable on the network with a known admin password."
}

zbuhj_render_linux_mac_note() {
  buh_section  "Linux and macOS"
  buh_line     "sshd is typically already installed and reachable. The operator's"
  buh_line     "manual scope is just: confirm sshd is running and the host is"
  buh_line     "reachable from the station. Garrison handles admin pubkey"
  buh_line     "placement and sshd hardening on first run via the password-"
  buh_line     "fallback path."
  buh_e
  buh_line     "If you are unsure of the admin user's password (or it has none),"
  buh_line     "set it to a known value first; garrison clears the password-"
  buh_line     "fallback path on first run, so the value stops affecting SSH:"
  buh_e
  buh_code     "sudo passwd <admin-user>"
  buh_e
  buh_line     "Operators who prefer to pre-establish key trust manually may use"
  buh_line     "the standard recipe instead; garrison's first run then converges"
  buh_line     "as a no-op on the admin pubkey:"
  buh_e
  buh_code     "ssh-copy-id -i ~/.ssh/<admin-pubkey>.pub <admin-user>@<host>"
}

zbuhj_render_windows_bootstrap() {
  buh_section  "Windows: sshd Reachability"
  buh_line     "All steps run on the Windows host in an elevated PowerShell."
  buh_line     "Right-click Start, Terminal (Admin), or search 'PowerShell' and"
  buh_line     "Run as Administrator."
  buh_e
  buh_section  "Preconditions:"
  buh_line     "- Windows host with administrator access"
  buh_line     "- Network reachable on TCP/${BUBC_windows_ssh_port} from operator's station"
  buh_e

  buh_step1    "Set or Confirm Admin Password:"
  buh_line     "Garrison's first run authenticates as the admin user via password"
  buh_line     "(once) before installing the pubkey. If you already know your local"
  buh_line     "admin password, skip. Otherwise set it to a known value:"
  buh_code     "net user <admin-user> <temp-password>"
  buh_line     "After garrison runs, sshd is hardened to key-only and this value"
  buh_line     "stops affecting SSH (it remains your Windows logon password — leave"
  buh_line     "it or reset to taste)."
  buh_e

  buh_step1    "Install OpenSSH Server:"
  buh_line     "Downloads from Windows Update; may take 10+ minutes."
  buh_code     "Add-WindowsCapability -Online -Name OpenSSH.Server~~~~0.0.1.0"
  buh_e

  buh_step1    "Start and Enable the Service:"
  buh_line     "First start also generates the default sshd_config."
  buh_code     "Start-Service sshd"
  buh_code     "Set-Service -Name sshd -StartupType Automatic"
  buh_e

  buyy_cmd_yawp "${BUBC_windows_sshd_config}"; local -r z_sshd_config_yelp="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buh_step1    "Enable Password Authentication in sshd_config:"
  buh_line     "Windows OpenSSH ships with sshd_config in a state that blocks"
  buh_line     "garrison's first-run password fallback (the wire protocol"
  buh_line     "advertises keyboard-interactive but the server refuses to"
  buh_line     "actually prompt). Open sshd_config, find the"
  buh_line     "PasswordAuthentication directive (commented or otherwise),"
  buh_line     "and set it to exactly:"
  buh_e
  buh_code     "PasswordAuthentication yes"
  buh_e
  buh_line     "If no PasswordAuthentication line exists, add one. Save"
  buh_line     "(notepad inherits your elevated context so SYSTEM-owned writes"
  buh_line     "succeed). Garrison flips this back to 'no' as part of its"
  buh_line     "hardening step, so this is a temporary state."
  buh_line     "File: ${z_sshd_config_yelp}"
  buh_e
  buh_code     "notepad ${BUBC_windows_sshd_config}"
  buh_code     "Restart-Service sshd"
  buh_e

  buh_step1    "Allow Port ${BUBC_windows_ssh_port} Through Firewall:"
  buh_code     "New-NetFirewallRule -Name ${BUBC_windows_fw_rule_name} -DisplayName \"${BUBC_windows_fw_display_name}\" -Enabled True -Direction Inbound -Protocol TCP -Action Allow -LocalPort ${BUBC_windows_ssh_port}"
  buh_e

  buh_section  "Verify Reachability"
  buh_line     "From the operator's station, confirm sshd answers (a password"
  buh_line     "prompt is expected and correct — garrison's first run will"
  buh_line     "type it through to ssh on /dev/tty):"
  buh_e
  buh_code     "ssh <admin-user>@<windows-host>"
}

zbuhj_render_post_bootstrap() {
  buh_section  "Run Garrison"
  buh_line     "With sshd reachable, run garrison for the chosen workload shell."
  buh_line     "Garrison establishes admin trust on its first run (typing the"
  buh_line     "admin password once when ssh prompts on /dev/tty), then destroys"
  buh_line     "any prior workload account and provisions a fresh one named"
  buh_line     "BURC_WORKLOAD_USER. Subsequent runs use key auth automatically."
  buh_e
  buh_line     "  buw-jpgb <investiture>  — native bash workload (Linux, macOS)"
  buh_line     "  buw-jpgc <investiture>  — Cygwin bash workload (Windows)"
  buh_line     "  buw-jpgw <investiture>  — WSL bash workload (Windows)"
  buh_e
  buh_line     "Choose c or w for Windows by which workload runtime you need."
  buh_line     "On Windows, both share this same admin SSH bootstrap; the"
  buh_line     "shell-letter only routes garrison's own admin session."
  buh_e
  buh_line     "After garrison succeeds, dispatch work via the workload"
  buh_line     "tabtargets:"
  buh_line     "  buw-jwk <investiture>           — probe workload reachability"
  buh_line     "  buw-jwc <investiture> <file>    — run a command file"
  buh_line     "  buw-jws <investiture>           — interactive workload session"
}

######################################################################
# External Functions (buhj_*)

buhj_top() {
  zbuhj_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Display jurisdiction handbook landing + sshd-reachability procedures"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  zbuhj_render_landing
  buh_e
  zbuhj_render_linux_mac_note
  buh_e
  zbuhj_render_windows_bootstrap
  buh_e
  zbuhj_render_post_bootstrap
}

# eof

1	//axhob_operation
2	{bust_handbook_jurisdiction_top}
3	
4	NOTE: Top-level landing surface for the {bus_jurisdiction} handbook.
5	Renders handbook prose to the operator's terminal as the entry point
6	into bootstrap, garrison, and dispatch procedures.
7	No remote contact and no profile mutation.
8	
9	//axhos_step
10	. *Render landing*: emit the jurisdiction handbook landing content,
11	including pointers to the admin SSH bootstrap procedure (operator-
12	manual prerequisite to {busn_garrison}), the per-shell-letter
13	garrison tabtargets, and the workload dispatch tabtargets.
14	
15	//axhog_guarantee
16	No remote contact occurred and no local file was written.
17	
18	//axhoc_completion
19	Exit 0 after the landing content is printed.
20	Non-zero only when the handbook content cannot be located or rendered.
21

1	//axhob_operation
2	{bust_knock}
3	
4	NOTE: Probes the workload account's reachability via key-only SSH.
5	Used to confirm that a {burp_investiture}'s workload trust is currently
6	usable before dispatching real work, and used internally as the
7	final-step validation of {busn_garrison}.
8	
9	//axhopt_typed_parameter
10	{burp_investiture}
11	
12	//axhoq_precondition
13	A {burp_regime} profile exists at the given {burp_investiture} and its
14	referenced {burn_regime} profile is reachable.
15	
16	//axhoq_precondition
17	{busn_garrison} has been run for this {burp_investiture} so that
18	{burc_workload_user} exists on the node and trusts the workload pubkey
19	derived from {burp_workload_key_file}.
20	
21	//axhos_step

34	Non-zero on connection refusal, authentication failure, remote-exec

input.content:
//axhob_operation
{bust_fenestrate}

NOTE: {busn_fenestrate} ceremony — Windows OpenSSH only. Establishes
admin SSH key trust and hardens `sshd_config` to key-only
authentication on the node, leaving it ready for {busn_garrison} to
provision the workload account. Two-phase remote-run because
`Restart-Service sshd` terminates its own ssh session: phase 1 places
admin trust, writes the hardened `sshd_config`, validates it, and
restarts sshd; phase 2 reconnects via key auth alone to verify the
post-restart state.

//axhopt_typed_parameter
{burp_investiture}

//axhoq_precondition
A {burp_regime} profile exists at the given {burp_investiture} and its
referenced {burn_regime} profile targets a Windows OpenSSH node.

//axhoq_precondition
The Windows handbook bootstrap is complete: OpenSSH Server installed,
sshd started and set to Automatic, firewall opens the configured SSH
port, and `sshd_config` carries `PasswordAuthentication yes` so that
fenestrate's phase-1 admin SSH session can fall through to a password
prompt on a fresh node.

//axhoq_precondition
{burp_privileged_user} is a member of the Administrators group with a
known password (typed once on phase-1 first run when ssh prompts on
/dev/tty; fenestrate itself never sees the password).

//axhoq_precondition
{burp_privileged_key_file} is unencrypted and exists on the operator's
station.

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 — open admin SSH*: connect as {burp_privileged_user} with
`PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password`. Steady-state:
{burp_privileged_key_file} authenticates by key. First run: ssh
prompts on /dev/tty for the admin password; operator types it once.

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 — place admin trust*: idempotently install the admin pubkey
(derived from {burp_privileged_key_file} via `ssh-keygen -y`) in
Windows OpenSSH `administrators_authorized_keys` as a bare key entry
(no `command=` directive — fenestrate is uniform across shell-letters).
Re-runs converge as a no-op when the line is already present. Performed
before any sshd_config change so that key auth is available for
phase 2's reconnect.

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 — lock down administrators_authorized_keys*: apply
`icacls` so only SYSTEM and the Administrators group hold ACL
entries; Windows OpenSSH refuses to read the file otherwise.
Idempotent.

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 — write hardened directives*: set `PubkeyAuthentication yes`,
`PasswordAuthentication no`, `PermitEmptyPasswords no` in
`sshd_config`. Idempotent — already-hardened configs converge to the
same byte sequence on re-run.

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 — verify written state*: PowerShell `Get-Content` returns
the raw `sshd_config` bytes; bash-side BCG-compliant matching extracts
each effective directive value and asserts it matches the intended
hardened value. Keeps PowerShell out of the parsing business (its
error semantics are unreliable).

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 — validate sshd_config*: `sshd -t` runs as the penultimate
atomic op of phase 1. A malformed config aborts fenestrate before it
could brick the running sshd.

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 — restart sshd*: `Restart-Service sshd` is the final atomic
op of phase 1. The bash orchestrator treats the disconnect immediately
after this exec as the expected phase boundary, not a failure.

//axhos_step
. *Phase 2 — reconnect via key auth*: re-establish admin SSH as
{burp_privileged_user} with `PreferredAuthentications=publickey`
(no password fallback). Failure here surfaces a brick — the operator's
manual password no longer opens a session.

//axhos_step
. *Phase 2 — verify post-state*: confirm the post-restart admin
session authenticates by key and the running sshd serves the hardened
configuration.

//axhog_guarantee
On success the node has admin SSH hardened to key-only with the
operator's admin pubkey installed in `administrators_authorized_keys`
under correct ACLs, `sshd_config` carries the three hardening
directives, and the post-restart admin session has been verified to
authenticate by key without password fallback.

//axhoc_completion
Exit 0 when phase 2 verification succeeds.
Non-zero on phase-1 admin SSH failure, pubkey-write failure, ACL
lockdown failure, sshd_config write failure, parsed-state mismatch,
`sshd -t` failure, or phase-2 key-auth failure.

1467	
1468	== Remote Node Access
1469	
1470	BUK reaches remote nodes under the
1471	{bus_jurisdiction}
1472	umbrella: a single station user provisions one workload account per
1473	node and dispatches commands to it. Privileged trust is the operator's
1474	prerequisite, not BUK's deliverable — first-time admin SSH bootstrap
1475	is an out-of-band handbook procedure.
1476	
1477	[[bus_jurisdiction]]
1478	{bus_jurisdiction}::
1479	The BUK feature-area for reaching remote nodes deterministically:
1480	operator-managed admin trust as input,
1481	{busn_garrison}
1482	to provision and refresh a workload account, and the workload-side
1483	tabtargets ({bust_knock}, {bust_command_file}, {bust_interactive_session})
1484	to dispatch work.
1485	+
1486	{bus_jurisdiction}
1487	is logical only — there is no AXLA-typed entity behind the umbrella.
1488	Its implementation seat is
1489	{bujb_jurisdiction}.
1490	
1491	[[bujb_jurisdiction]]
1492	{bujb_jurisdiction}::
1493	The BCG-compliant module
1494	(`Tools/buk/bujb_jurisdiction.sh` plus its `bujb_cli.sh` companion)
1495	that hardcodes the
1496	{bus_jurisdiction}
1497	contracts not expressed in regime data:
1498	the three shell-letter→`command=` directive mappings
1499	(`b` = native bash for Linux/Mac;
1500	`c` = Cygwin;
1501	`w` = WSL),
1502	the workload private-key destination paths on the remote per shell-letter,
1503	and the canonical WSL distribution name (`rbtww-main`).
1504	+
1505	SSH transport mechanics live in code;
1506	{burp_regime}
1507	carries no shell selection.
1508	
1509	=== Privileges
1510	
1511	A
1512	{burn_regime}
1513	instance lives under `.buk/rbmn_nodes/<{burn_viceroyalty}>/` and is shared
1514	across station users (git-transported); it carries node shape only and
1515	no key material.
1516	A
1517	{burp_regime}
1518	instance lives under `.buk/rbmu_users/<{burs_user}>/<{burp_investiture}>/`;
1519	the
1520	{burs_user}
1521	owns its credential subtree exclusively, and operations write only
1522	under the current station user's tree.
1523	
1524	Garrison authenticates as
1525	{burp_privileged_user}
1526	using
1527	{burp_privileged_key_file}
1528	(key auth only — no password fallback).
1529	Workload account is unprivileged, ssh-only, and named by
1530	{burc_workload_user}
1531	(project-wide convention, single source of truth).
1532	
1533	=== Garrison-Destructive Model
1534	
1535	Every garrison invocation wipes prior workload state on the node
1536	regardless of which
1537	{burp_investiture}
1538	or station user runs it.
1539	There is exactly one workload user per node, ever; node runtime state
1540	belongs to whoever last garrisoned it.
1541	Garrison subsumes lifecycle: there is no separate verb to materialize,
1542	remove, or enumerate workload accounts — every garrison rebuilds from a
1543	clean slate.

file.content:
[[burx_ended_at]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string axd_optional
{burx_ended_at}::
Nanosecond-precision timestamp when dispatch completed.
Same format as
{burx_began_at}.
Present only when
{burx_exit_status}
is present.

include::BUSD-DispatchRuntime.adoc[]

== Remote Node Access

BUK reaches remote nodes under the
{bus_jurisdiction}
umbrella: a single station user provisions one workload account per
node and dispatches commands to it. Privileged trust is the operator's
prerequisite, not BUK's deliverable — first-time admin SSH bootstrap
is an out-of-band handbook procedure.

[[bus_jurisdiction]]
{bus_jurisdiction}::
The BUK feature-area for reaching remote nodes deterministically:
operator-managed admin trust as input,
{busn_garrison}
to provision and refresh a workload account, and the workload-side
tabtargets ({bust_knock}, {bust_command_file}, {bust_interactive_session})
to dispatch work.
+
{bus_jurisdiction}
is logical only — there is no AXLA-typed entity behind the umbrella.
Its implementation seat is
{bujb_jurisdiction}.

[[bujb_jurisdiction]]
{bujb_jurisdiction}::
The BCG-compliant module
(`Tools/buk/bujb_jurisdiction.sh` plus its `bujb_cli.sh` companion)
that hardcodes the
{bus_jurisdiction}
contracts not expressed in regime data:
the three shell-letter→`command=` directive mappings
(`b` = native bash for Linux/Mac;
`c` = Cygwin;
`w` = WSL),
the workload private-key destination paths on the remote per shell-letter,
and the canonical WSL distribution name (`rbtww-main`).
+
SSH transport mechanics live in code;
{burp_regime}
carries no shell selection.

=== Privileges

A
{burn_regime}
instance lives under `.buk/rbmn_nodes/<{burn_viceroyalty}>/` and is shared
across station users (git-transported); it carries node shape only and
no key material.
A
{burp_regime}
instance lives under `.buk/rbmu_users/<{burs_user}>/<{burp_investiture}>/`;
the
{burs_user}
owns its credential subtree exclusively, and operations write only
under the current station user's tree.

Garrison authenticates as
{burp_privileged_user}
using
{burp_privileged_key_file}
(key auth only — no password fallback).
Workload account is unprivileged, ssh-only, and named by
{burc_workload_user}
(project-wide convention, single source of truth).

=== Garrison-Destructive Model

Every garrison invocation wipes prior workload state on the node
regardless of which
{burp_investiture}
or station user runs it.
There is exactly one workload user per node, ever; node runtime state
belongs to whoever last garrisoned it.
Garrison subsumes lifecycle: there is no separate verb to materialize,
remove, or enumerate workload accounts — every garrison rebuilds from a
clean slate.

=== Tabtarget Catalog

[[bus_jurisdiction]]
{bus_jurisdiction}::
The BUK feature-area for reaching remote nodes deterministically.
Two verbs cover the privileged-side ceremonies:
{busn_fenestrate}
hardens admin SSH trust and `sshd_config` on Windows OpenSSH nodes
(operator-manual on Linux/Mac, e.g., `ssh-copy-id`); {busn_garrison}
provisions and refreshes a workload account from an existing admin
SSH foothold. The workload-side tabtargets
({bust_knock}, {bust_command_file}, {bust_interactive_session})
dispatch work to that account.
+
{bus_jurisdiction}
is logical only — there is no AXLA-typed entity behind the umbrella.
Its implementation seat is
{bujb_jurisdiction}.

-prerequisite, not BUK's deliverable — first-time admin SSH bootstrap
+umbrella: a single station user establishes admin SSH trust on the
+node, provisions one workload account, and dispatches commands to it.

input.old_string:
=== Garrison-Destructive Model

Every garrison invocation wipes prior workload state on the node
regardless of which
{burp_investiture}
or station user runs it.
There is exactly one workload user per node, ever; node runtime state
belongs to whoever last garrisoned it.
Garrison subsumes lifecycle: there is no separate verb to materialize,
remove, or enumerate workload accounts — every garrison rebuilds from a
clean slate.
input.new_string:
=== Garrison-Destructive Model

Every
{busn_garrison}
invocation wipes prior workload state on the node regardless of which
{burp_investiture}
or station user runs it.
There is exactly one workload user per node, ever; node runtime state
belongs to whoever last garrisoned it.
Garrison subsumes the workload-account lifecycle: there is no separate
verb to materialize, remove, or enumerate workload accounts — every
garrison rebuilds from a clean slate.
+
Admin-side sshd configuration is out of garrison's scope.
{busn_fenestrate}
owns Windows OpenSSH `sshd_config` hardening; on Linux/Mac the
operator manages sshd directly.

1563	=== Tabtarget Catalog
1564	
1565	Thirteen tabtargets cover regime configuration, garrison ceremony, and
1566	workload dispatch.
1567	PowerShell garrison and dispatch are deferred and not enumerated below.
1568	
1569	==== Regime Configuration
1570	
1571	[[bust_node_regime_list]]
1572	//axvo_tabtarget
1573	{bust_node_regime_list}::
1574	`buw-rnl` —
1575	List all {burn_regime} profiles under `.buk/rbmn_nodes/`.
1576	
1577	[[bust_node_regime_render]]
1578	//axvo_tabtarget
1579	{bust_node_regime_render}::
1580	`buw-rnr` —
1581	Render a single {burn_regime} profile
1582	(viceroyalty passed as positional argument).
1583	
1584	[[bust_node_regime_validate]]
1585	//axvo_tabtarget
1586	{bust_node_regime_validate}::
1587	`buw-rnv` —
1588	Validate a single {burn_regime} profile
1589	(viceroyalty passed as positional argument).
1590	
1591	[[bust_privilege_regime_list]]
1592	//axvo_tabtarget
1593	{bust_privilege_regime_list}::
1594	`buw-rpl` —
1595	List all {burp_regime} profiles for the current {burs_user}.
1596	
1597	[[bust_privilege_regime_render]]
1598	//axvo_tabtarget
1599	{bust_privilege_regime_render}::
1600	`buw-rpr` —
1601	Render a single {burp_regime} profile
1602	(investiture passed as positional argument).
1603	
1604	[[bust_privilege_regime_validate]]
1605	//axvo_tabtarget
1606	{bust_privilege_regime_validate}::
1607	`buw-rpv` —
1608	Validate a single {burp_regime} profile
1609	(investiture passed as positional argument).
1610	
1611	==== Garrison (per shell-letter)
1612	
1613	[[bust_garrison_bash]]
1614	//axvo_tabtarget
1615	{bust_garrison_bash}::
1616	`buw-jpgb` —
1617	{busn_garrison} ceremony with native bash as the workload shell
1618	(Linux and macOS nodes).
1619	
1620	[[bust_garrison_cygwin]]
1621	//axvo_tabtarget
1622	{bust_garrison_cygwin}::
1623	`buw-jpgc` —
1624	{busn_garrison} ceremony with Cygwin bash as the workload shell
1625	(Windows nodes via Cygwin sshd).
1626	
1627	[[bust_garrison_wsl]]
1628	//axvo_tabtarget
1629	{bust_garrison_wsl}::
1630	`buw-jpgw` —
1631	{busn_garrison} ceremony with WSL bash as the workload shell
1632	(Windows nodes via WSL distribution `rbtww-main`).
1633	
1634	==== Workload Dispatch
1635	
1636	[[bust_knock]]
1637	//axvo_tabtarget
1638	{bust_knock}::
1639	`buw-jwk` —
1640	Probe workload reachability via key-only SSH;
1641	returns 0 if the workload account answers a no-op command.
1642	
1643	[[bust_command_file]]
1644	//axvo_tabtarget
1645	{bust_command_file}::
1646	`buw-jwc` —
1647	Run the contents of a local command file as the workload account;
1648	shell is determined by whichever shell-letter garrisoned the node.
1649	Captures stdout, stderr, and remote exit status to {burd_output_dir}.
1650	
1651	[[bust_interactive_session]]
1652	//axvo_tabtarget
1653	{bust_interactive_session}::
1654	`buw-jws` —
1655	Open an interactive SSH session to the workload account.
1656	
1657	==== Handbook
1658	
1659	[[bust_handbook_jurisdiction_top]]
1660	//axvo_tabtarget
1661	{bust_handbook_jurisdiction_top}::
1662	`buw-hj0` —
1663	Top-level handbook landing for jurisdiction procedures
1664	(garrison preparation, bootstrap pointers, dispatch usage).
1665	
1666	=== Verb Definitions
1667	
1668	[[busn_garrison]]
1669	//axl_voices axt_command
1670	{busn_garrison}::
1671	Establish or refresh workload trust on a node from an existing
1672	admin SSH foothold.
1673	A single tabtarget per shell-letter
1674	(`buw-jpgb`, `buw-jpgc`, `buw-jpgw`)
1675	runs the same destructive ceremony with the shell-letter selecting
1676	remote shell mappings via
1677	{bujb_jurisdiction}.
1678	Idempotent on re-run; destructive of any prior workload state.
1679	+
1680	Ceremony steps:
1681	+
1682	. SSH as
1683	{burp_privileged_user}
1684	using
1685	{burp_privileged_key_file};
1686	key-auth only.
1687	. Idempotently place admin pubkey
1688	(derived from privkey via `ssh-keygen -y`)
1689	in admin's authorized_keys with the shell-letter's `command=` directive.
1690	. Destroy any existing workload account named
1691	{burc_workload_user}
1692	via `userdel -r` or platform equivalent — removes account and home
1693	directory.
1694	. Create fresh workload account named
1695	{burc_workload_user}:
1696	unprivileged, no sudo, no admin group, ssh-only.
1697	. Place workload pubkey
1698	(derived from
1699	{burp_workload_key_file}
1700	via `ssh-keygen -y`)
1701	in workload's authorized_keys with the shell-letter's `command=`
1702	directive.
1703	. Copy
1704	{burp_workload_key_file}
1705	to the remote workload account at the path hardcoded in
1706	{bujb_jurisdiction}
1707	per shell-letter,
1708	so the workload can authenticate outbound (e.g., to GitHub).
1709	. Validate round-trip: SSH as workload, exec a no-op, verify exit 0.
1710	
1711	=== Input Artifacts
1712	
1713	[[busi_scribed_command_file]]
1714	//axl_voices axo_entity
1715	{busi_scribed_command_file}::
1716	A file authored by the operator containing commands to be executed
1717	on the remote node by {bust_command_file}.
1718	Path passed as a positional argument; contents are streamed
1719	to the remote shell at dispatch time.

verb to materialize, remove, or enumerate workload accounts — every
garrison rebuilds from a clean slate.
+
Admin-side sshd configuration is out of garrison's scope.
{busn_fenestrate}
owns Windows OpenSSH `sshd_config` hardening; on Linux/Mac the
operator manages sshd directly.

=== Tabtarget Catalog

Thirteen tabtargets cover regime configuration, garrison ceremony, and
workload dispatch.
PowerShell garrison and dispatch are deferred and not enumerated below.

==== Regime Configuration

[[bust_node_regime_list]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_node_regime_list}::
`buw-rnl` —
List all {burn_regime} profiles under `.buk/rbmn_nodes/`.

[[bust_node_regime_render]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_node_regime_render}::
`buw-rnr` —
Render a single {burn_regime} profile
(viceroyalty passed as positional argument).

[[bust_node_regime_validate]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_node_regime_validate}::
`buw-rnv` —
Validate a single {burn_regime} profile
(viceroyalty passed as positional argument).

[[bust_privilege_regime_list]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_privilege_regime_list}::
`buw-rpl` —
List all {burp_regime} profiles for the current {burs_user}.

[[bust_privilege_regime_render]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_privilege_regime_render}::
`buw-rpr` —
Render a single {burp_regime} profile
(investiture passed as positional argument).

[[bust_privilege_regime_validate]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_privilege_regime_validate}::
`buw-rpv` —
Validate a single {burp_regime} profile
(investiture passed as positional argument).

==== Garrison (per shell-letter)

[[bust_garrison_bash]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_garrison_bash}::
`buw-jpgb` —
{busn_garrison} ceremony with native bash as the workload shell
(Linux and macOS nodes).

[[bust_garrison_cygwin]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_garrison_cygwin}::
`buw-jpgc` —
{busn_garrison} ceremony with Cygwin bash as the workload shell
(Windows nodes via Cygwin sshd).

[[bust_garrison_wsl]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_garrison_wsl}::
`buw-jpgw` —
{busn_garrison} ceremony with WSL bash as the workload shell
(Windows nodes via WSL distribution `rbtww-main`).

==== Workload Dispatch

[[bust_knock]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_knock}::
`buw-jwk` —
Probe workload reachability via key-only SSH;
returns 0 if the workload account answers a no-op command.

[[bust_command_file]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_command_file}::
`buw-jwc` —
Run the contents of a local command file as the workload account;
shell is determined by whichever shell-letter garrisoned the node.
Captures stdout, stderr, and remote exit status to {burd_output_dir}.

[[bust_interactive_session]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_interactive_session}::
`buw-jws` —
Open an interactive SSH session to the workload account.

==== Handbook

[[bust_handbook_jurisdiction_top]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_handbook_jurisdiction_top}::
`buw-hj0` —
Top-level handbook landing for jurisdiction procedures
(garrison preparation, bootstrap pointers, dispatch usage).

=== Verb Definitions

[[busn_garrison]]
//axl_voices axt_command
{busn_garrison}::
Establish or refresh workload trust on a node from an existing
admin SSH foothold.
A single tabtarget per shell-letter
(`buw-jpgb`, `buw-jpgc`, `buw-jpgw`)
runs the same destructive ceremony with the shell-letter selecting
remote shell mappings via
{bujb_jurisdiction}.
Idempotent on re-run; destructive of any prior workload state.
+
Ceremony steps:
+
. SSH as
{burp_privileged_user}
using
{burp_privileged_key_file};
key-auth only.
. Idempotently place admin pubkey
(derived from privkey via `ssh-keygen -y`)
in admin's authorized_keys with the shell-letter's `command=` directive.
. Destroy any existing workload account named
{burc_workload_user}
via `userdel -r` or platform equivalent — removes account and home
directory.
. Create fresh workload account named
{burc_workload_user}:
unprivileged, no sudo, no admin group, ssh-only.
. Place workload pubkey
(derived from
{burp_workload_key_file}
via `ssh-keygen -y`)
in workload's authorized_keys with the shell-letter's `command=`
directive.
. Copy
{burp_workload_key_file}
to the remote workload account at the path hardcoded in
{bujb_jurisdiction}
per shell-letter,
so the workload can authenticate outbound (e.g., to GitHub).
. Validate round-trip: SSH as workload, exec a no-op, verify exit 0.

=== Input Artifacts

[[busi_scribed_command_file]]
//axl_voices axo_entity
{busi_scribed_command_file}::
A file authored by the operator containing commands to be executed
on the remote node by {bust_command_file}.
Path passed as a positional argument; contents are streamed
to the remote shell at dispatch time.

include::BUSTLL-NodeRegimeList.adoc[]

include::BUSTLR-NodeRegimeRender.adoc[]

include::BUSTLV-NodeRegimeValidate.adoc[]

include::BUSTPL-PrivilegeRegimeList.adoc[]

include::BUSTPR-PrivilegeRegimeRender.adoc[]

toolUseResult.oldString:
=== Verb Definitions

[[busn_garrison]]
//axl_voices axt_command
{busn_garrison}::
Establish or refresh workload trust on a node from an existing
admin SSH foothold.
A single tabtarget per shell-letter
(`buw-jpgb`, `buw-jpgc`, `buw-jpgw`)
runs the same destructive ceremony with the shell-letter selecting
remote shell mappings via
{bujb_jurisdiction}.
Idempotent on re-run; destructive of any prior workload state.
+
Ceremony steps:
+
. SSH as
{burp_privileged_user}
using
{burp_privileged_key_file};
key-auth only.
. Idempotently place admin pubkey
(derived from privkey via `ssh-keygen -y`)
in admin's authorized_keys with the shell-letter's `command=` directive.
. Destroy any existing workload account named
{burc_workload_user}
via `userdel -r` or platform equivalent — removes account and home
directory.
. Create fresh workload account named
{burc_workload_user}:
unprivileged, no sudo, no admin group, ssh-only.
. Place workload pubkey
(derived from
{burp_workload_key_file}
via `ssh-keygen -y`)
in workload's authorized_keys with the shell-letter's `command=`
directive.
. Copy
{burp_workload_key_file}
to the remote workload account at the path hardcoded in
{bujb_jurisdiction}
per shell-letter,
so the workload can authenticate outbound (e.g., to GitHub).
. Validate round-trip: SSH as workload, exec a no-op, verify exit 0.
toolUseResult.newString:
=== Verb Definitions

[[busn_fenestrate]]
//axl_voices axt_command
{busn_fenestrate}::
Establish admin SSH key trust and harden Windows OpenSSH
`sshd_config` to key-only on a node, leaving it ready for
{busn_garrison}.
Uniform across shell-letters — one tabtarget (`buw-jpF`).
Targets Windows OpenSSH only; Linux/Mac sshd hardening is
operator-manual outside BUK.
Idempotent on re-run.
+
Two-phase ceremony forced by `Restart-Service sshd` terminating its
own ssh session:
+
* Phase 1: SSH as
{burp_privileged_user}
with `PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password` (one-time password
fallback on a fresh node);
idempotently place the admin pubkey
(derived from
{burp_privileged_key_file}
via `ssh-keygen -y`)
in `administrators_authorized_keys` as a bare entry (no `command=`);
apply `icacls` lockdown;
write the hardening directives to `sshd_config`
(`PubkeyAuthentication yes`,
`PasswordAuthentication no`,
`PermitEmptyPasswords no`);
verify written state via PowerShell `Get-Content` + bash-side
parsing;
run `sshd -t` as the penultimate atomic op; restart sshd as the
final atomic op.
* Phase 2: reconnect using
{burp_privileged_key_file}
with `PreferredAuthentications=publickey` (no password fallback);
verify the post-restart admin session authenticates by key alone.
+
The bash orchestrator treats the disconnect immediately after
`Restart-Service sshd` as the expected phase boundary, not a failure.

[[busn_garrison]]
//axl_voices axt_command
{busn_garrison}::
Provision or refresh the workload account on a node from an existing
admin SSH foothold.
A single tabtarget per shell-letter
(`buw-jpGb`, `buw-jpGc`, `buw-jpGw`)
runs the same destructive ceremony with the shell-letter selecting
remote shell mappings via
{bujb_jurisdiction}.
Garrison never reads or writes any `sshd_config` and never alters
admin authorized_keys; first-step admin SSH must succeed by key auth
alone, surfacing any missing admin trust immediately.
Idempotent on re-run; destructive of any prior workload state.
+
Ceremony steps:
+
. SSH as
{burp_privileged_user}
using
{burp_privileged_key_file};
key-auth only (no password fallback). On Windows, this presupposes
that
{busn_fenestrate}
has run for this {burp_investiture}.
. Destroy any existing workload account named
{burc_workload_user}
via `userdel -r` or platform equivalent — removes account and home
directory.
. Create fresh workload account named
{burc_workload_user}:
unprivileged, no sudo, no admin group, ssh-only.
. Place workload pubkey
(derived from
{burp_workload_key_file}
via `ssh-keygen -y`)
in workload's authorized_keys with the shell-letter's `command=`
directive.
. Copy
{burp_workload_key_file}
to the remote workload account at the path hardcoded in
{bujb_jurisdiction}
per shell-letter,
so the workload can authenticate outbound (e.g., to GitHub).
. Validate round-trip: SSH as workload, exec a no-op, verify exit 0.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
//axhob_operation
{bust_garrison_bash}

NOTE: {busn_garrison} ceremony with native bash as the workload shell,
for Linux and macOS nodes. Dual-purpose: the front-half (steps 1-4)
idempotently establishes admin SSH trust and hardens sshd; the
back-half (steps 5-9) destroys any prior workload account named
{burc_workload_user}
and recreates it from a clean slate.

//axhopt_typed_parameter
{burp_investiture}

//axhoq_precondition
A {burp_regime} profile exists at the given {burp_investiture} and its
referenced {burn_regime} profile is reachable.

//axhoq_precondition
sshd is reachable on the node and {burp_privileged_user} can authenticate
either via {burp_privileged_key_file} (steady-state) or via password
(first run only). Garrison opens its admin SSH session with
`PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password`, so on a fresh node
ssh prompts for the admin password on /dev/tty; garrison itself never
sees the password.

//axhoq_precondition
{burp_workload_key_file} is unencrypted and exists on the operator's
station.

//axhos_step
. *Open admin SSH*: connect as {burp_privileged_user} with
`PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password`. Steady-state:
{burp_privileged_key_file} authenticates. First run: ssh prompts on
/dev/tty for the admin password; operator types it once.

//axhos_step
. *Place admin trust*: idempotently install the admin pubkey
(derived from {burp_privileged_key_file} via `ssh-keygen -y`) in
admin's authorized_keys with the shell-letter `b` `command=` directive
from {bujb_jurisdiction}. Re-runs converge as a no-op when the line
is already present.

//axhos_step
. *Lock down admin authorized_keys*: `chmod 600` and `chown` to the
admin user. Idempotent.

//axhos_step
. *Harden sshd_config*: ensure `PubkeyAuthentication yes`,
`PasswordAuthentication no`, `PermitEmptyPasswords no`. Validate
(`sshd -t`) and restart sshd. Idempotent — already-hardened configs
re-converge to the same state and the restart is a no-op effect on
established sessions. After this step the password-fallback path used
in step 1 is closed; subsequent runs authenticate by key.

//axhos_step
. *Destroy workload*: remove any existing {burc_workload_user} account
via `userdel -r`.

//axhos_step
. *Create workload*: provision {burc_workload_user} as an unprivileged,
ssh-only account.

//axhos_step
. *Place workload trust*: write the workload pubkey
(derived via `ssh-keygen -y`) to the workload's authorized_keys with
the shell-letter `b` `command=` directive.

//axhos_step
. *Plant workload key*: copy {burp_workload_key_file} to the workload
account at the shell-letter `b` destination path hardcoded in
{bujb_jurisdiction}.

//axhos_step
. *Validate round-trip*: SSH as workload, run a no-op command, verify
exit 0.

//axhog_guarantee
On success the node has admin SSH hardened to key-only with the
operator's admin pubkey carrying the shell-letter `b` `command=`
directive, and exactly one workload account named
{burc_workload_user}, ssh-only, with both inbound (from operator) and
outbound (to GitHub or equivalent) key trust in place.

//axhoc_completion
Exit 0 when the round-trip validation succeeds.
Non-zero on admin SSH failure, sshd_config validation or restart
failure, account-management failure, key-write failure, or workload
validation failure.

# Bash Utility Handbook - Jurisdiction Procedures
#
# Handbook content for BUK's remote-node feature area (BUS0 §Remote
# Node Access). Garrison handles first-run admin trust establishment
# itself (password-fallback on /dev/tty, then key-only thereafter);
# the operator's manual scope is just making sshd reachable on the
# network with a known admin password.
input.new_string:
# Bash Utility Handbook - Jurisdiction Procedures
#
# Handbook content for BUK's remote-node feature area (BUS0 §Remote
# Node Access). On Windows, fenestrate handles first-run admin trust
# establishment and sshd_config hardening (password-fallback on
# /dev/tty during its phase-1 admin session, then key-only
# thereafter); garrison handles workload account provisioning. The
# operator's manual scope is just making sshd reachable on the
# network with a known admin password. Linux/Mac admin trust is
# operator-manual (e.g., ssh-copy-id); BUK ships no fenestrate for
# non-Windows-OpenSSH nodes.

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Bash Utility Handbook - Jurisdiction Procedures
#
# Handbook content for BUK's remote-node feature area (BUS0 §Remote
# Node Access). Garrison handles first-run admin trust establishment
# itself (password-fallback on /dev/tty, then key-only thereafter);
# the operator's manual scope is just making sshd reachable on the
# network with a known admin password.

set -euo pipefail

test -z "${ZBUHJ_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module buhj multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZBUHJ_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Internal: Kindle and Sentinel

zbuhj_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZBUHJ_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module buhj already kindled"

  test -n "${BUBC_windows_ssh_port:-}" || buc_die "buhj requires bubc_constants.sh sourced before kindle"

  readonly ZBUHJ_KINDLED=1
}

zbuhj_sentinel() {
  test "${ZBUHJ_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module buhj not kindled - call zbuhj_kindle first"
}

######################################################################
# Internal: Section renderers

zbuhj_render_landing() {
  buh_section  "Jurisdiction Handbook"
  buh_line     "BUK reaches remote nodes through Garrison: a destructive ceremony"
  buh_line     "that provisions one workload account per node, dispatched as the"
  buh_line     "current station user. Garrison never generates or modifies SSH"
  buh_line     "key material; the operator owns all key administration."
  buh_e
  buh_line     "Garrison handles first-run admin trust itself. Its admin SSH"
  buh_line     "session uses PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password — on a"
  buh_line     "fresh node ssh falls through to a password prompt on /dev/tty,"
  buh_line     "the operator types the admin password once, garrison installs"
  buh_line     "the admin pubkey + sshd hardening, and subsequent runs use key"
  buh_line     "auth automatically. The operator's manual scope reduces to:"
  buh_line     "make sshd reachable on the network with a known admin password."
}

zbuhj_render_linux_mac_note() {
  buh_section  "Linux and macOS"
  buh_line     "sshd is typically already installed and reachable. The operator's"
  buh_line     "manual scope is just: confirm sshd is running and the host is"
  buh_line     "reachable from the station. Garrison handles admin pubkey"
  buh_line     "placement and sshd hardening on first run via the password-"
  buh_line     "fallback path."
  buh_e
  buh_line     "If you are unsure of the admin user's password (or it has none),"
  buh_line     "set it to a known value first; garrison clears the password-"
  buh_line     "fallback path on first run, so the value stops affecting SSH:"
  buh_e
  buh_code     "sudo passwd <admin-user>"
  buh_e
  buh_line     "Operators who prefer to pre-establish key trust manually may use"
  buh_line     "the standard recipe instead; garrison's first run then converges"
  buh_line     "as a no-op on the admin pubkey:"
  buh_e
  buh_code     "ssh-copy-id -i ~/.ssh/<admin-pubkey>.pub <admin-user>@<host>"
}

zbuhj_render_windows_bootstrap() {
  buh_section  "Windows: sshd Reachability"
  buh_line     "All steps run on the Windows host in an elevated PowerShell."
  buh_line     "Right-click Start, Terminal (Admin), or search 'PowerShell' and"
  buh_line     "Run as Administrator."
  buh_e
  buh_section  "Preconditions:"
  buh_line     "- Windows host with administrator access"
  buh_line     "- Network reachable on TCP/${BUBC_windows_ssh_port} from operator's station"
  buh_e

  buh_step1    "Set or Confirm Admin Password:"
  buh_line     "Garrison's first run authenticates as the admin user via password"
  buh_line     "(once) before installing the pubkey. If you already know your local"
  buh_line     "admin password, skip. Otherwise set it to a known value:"
  buh_code     "net user <admin-user> <temp-password>"
  buh_line     "After garrison runs, sshd is hardened to key-only and this value"
  buh_line     "stops affecting SSH (it remains your Windows logon password — leave"
  buh_line     "it or reset to taste)."
  buh_e

  buh_step1    "Install OpenSSH Server:"
  buh_line     "Downloads from Windows Update; may take 10+ minutes."
  buh_code     "Add-WindowsCapability -Online -Name OpenSSH.Server~~~~0.0.1.0"
  buh_e

  buh_step1    "Start and Enable the Service:"
  buh_line     "First start also generates the default sshd_config."
  buh_code     "Start-Service sshd"
  buh_code     "Set-Service -Name sshd -StartupType Automatic"
  buh_e

  buyy_cmd_yawp "${BUBC_windows_sshd_config}"; local -r z_sshd_config_yelp="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buh_step1    "Enable Password Authentication in sshd_config:"
  buh_line     "Windows OpenSSH ships with sshd_config in a state that blocks"
  buh_line     "garrison's first-run password fallback (the wire protocol"
  buh_line     "advertises keyboard-interactive but the server refuses to"
  buh_line     "actually prompt). Open sshd_config, find the"
  buh_line     "PasswordAuthentication directive (commented or otherwise),"
  buh_line     "and set it to exactly:"
  buh_e
  buh_code     "PasswordAuthentication yes"
  buh_e
  buh_line     "If no PasswordAuthentication line exists, add one. Save"
  buh_line     "(notepad inherits your elevated context so SYSTEM-owned writes"
  buh_line     "succeed). Garrison flips this back to 'no' as part of its"
  buh_line     "hardening step, so this is a temporary state."
  buh_line     "File: ${z_sshd_config_yelp}"
  buh_e
  buh_code     "notepad ${BUBC_windows_sshd_config}"
  buh_code     "Restart-Service sshd"
  buh_e

  buh_step1    "Allow Port ${BUBC_windows_ssh_port} Through Firewall:"
  buh_code     "New-NetFirewallRule -Name ${BUBC_windows_fw_rule_name} -DisplayName \"${BUBC_windows_fw_display_name}\" -Enabled True -Direction Inbound -Protocol TCP -Action Allow -LocalPort ${BUBC_windows_ssh_port}"
  buh_e

  buh_section  "Verify Reachability"
  buh_line     "From the operator's station, confirm sshd answers (a password"
  buh_line     "prompt is expected and correct — garrison's first run will"
  buh_line     "type it through to ssh on /dev/tty):"
  buh_e
  buh_code     "ssh <admin-user>@<windows-host>"
}

zbuhj_render_post_bootstrap() {
  buh_section  "Run Garrison"
  buh_line     "With sshd reachable, run garrison for the chosen workload shell."
  buh_line     "Garrison establishes admin trust on its first run (typing the"
  buh_line     "admin password once when ssh prompts on /dev/tty), then destroys"
  buh_line     "any prior workload account and provisions a fresh one named"
  buh_line     "BURC_WORKLOAD_USER. Subsequent runs use key auth automatically."
  buh_e
  buh_line     "  buw-jpgb <investiture>  — native bash workload (Linux, macOS)"
  buh_line     "  buw-jpgc <investiture>  — Cygwin bash workload (Windows)"
  buh_line     "  buw-jpgw <investiture>  — WSL bash workload (Windows)"
  buh_e
  buh_line     "Choose c or w for Windows by which workload runtime you need."
  buh_line     "On Windows, both share this same admin SSH bootstrap; the"
  buh_line     "shell-letter only routes garrison's own admin session."
  buh_e
  buh_line     "After garrison succeeds, dispatch work via the workload"
  buh_line     "tabtargets:"
  buh_line     "  buw-jwk <investiture>           — probe workload reachability"
  buh_line     "  buw-jwc <investiture> <file>    — run a command file"
  buh_line     "  buw-jws <investiture>           — interactive workload session"
}

######################################################################
# External Functions (buhj_*)

buhj_top() {
  zbuhj_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Display jurisdiction handbook landing + sshd-reachability procedures"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  zbuhj_render_landing
  buh_e
  zbuhj_render_linux_mac_note
  buh_e
  zbuhj_render_windows_bootstrap
  buh_e
  zbuhj_render_post_bootstrap
}

zbuhj_render_landing() {
  buh_section  "Jurisdiction Handbook"
  buh_line     "BUK reaches remote nodes through two ceremonies. Fenestrate"
  buh_line     "(Windows OpenSSH only) hardens admin SSH trust and sshd_config"
  buh_line     "to key-only; Garrison provisions the workload account. BUK"
  buh_line     "never generates or modifies SSH key material; the operator owns"

  buh_line     "publickey,password, falls through to a /dev/tty password prompt"
  buh_line     "on a fresh node, the operator types the admin password once,"
  buh_line     "fenestrate installs the admin pubkey + sshd hardening + restarts"
  buh_line     "sshd, and phase 2 reconnects by key alone. Subsequent fenestrate"
  buh_line     "and garrison runs use key auth automatically. The operator's"

  buh_line     "directly against an existing key-trusted admin foothold."

  buh_line     "auth automatically. The operator's manual scope reduces to:"
  buh_line     "make sshd reachable on the network with a known admin password."

  buh_step1    "Set or Confirm Admin Password:"

  buh_step1    "Start and Enable the Service:"

  buh_code     "ssh <admin-user>@<windows-host>"

zbuhj_render_post_bootstrap() {
  buh_section  "Run Garrison"
  buh_line     "With sshd reachable, run garrison for the chosen workload shell."
  buh_line     "Garrison establishes admin trust on its first run (typing the"
  buh_line     "admin password once when ssh prompts on /dev/tty), then destroys"
  buh_line     "any prior workload account and provisions a fresh one named"
  buh_line     "BURC_WORKLOAD_USER. Subsequent runs use key auth automatically."
  buh_e
  buh_line     "  buw-jpgb <investiture>  — native bash workload (Linux, macOS)"
  buh_line     "  buw-jpgc <investiture>  — Cygwin bash workload (Windows)"
  buh_line     "  buw-jpgw <investiture>  — WSL bash workload (Windows)"

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Bash Utility Handbook - Jurisdiction Procedures
#
# Handbook content for BUK's remote-node feature area (BUS0 §Remote
# Node Access). On Windows, fenestrate handles first-run admin trust
# establishment and sshd_config hardening (password-fallback on
# /dev/tty during its phase-1 admin session, then key-only
# thereafter); garrison handles workload account provisioning. The
# operator's manual scope is just making sshd reachable on the
# network with a known admin password. Linux/Mac admin trust is
# operator-manual (e.g., ssh-copy-id); BUK ships no fenestrate for
# non-Windows-OpenSSH nodes.

set -euo pipefail

test -z "${ZBUHJ_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module buhj multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZBUHJ_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Internal: Kindle and Sentinel

zbuhj_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZBUHJ_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module buhj already kindled"

  test -n "${BUBC_windows_ssh_port:-}" || buc_die "buhj requires bubc_constants.sh sourced before kindle"

  readonly ZBUHJ_KINDLED=1
}

zbuhj_sentinel() {
  test "${ZBUHJ_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module buhj not kindled - call zbuhj_kindle first"
}

######################################################################
# Internal: Section renderers

zbuhj_render_landing() {
  buh_section  "Jurisdiction Handbook"
  buh_line     "BUK reaches remote nodes through two ceremonies. Fenestrate"
  buh_line     "(Windows OpenSSH only) hardens admin SSH trust and sshd_config"
  buh_line     "to key-only; Garrison provisions the workload account. BUK"
  buh_line     "never generates or modifies SSH key material; the operator owns"
  buh_line     "all key administration."
  buh_e
  buh_line     "On Windows, fenestrate handles first-run admin trust itself: its"
  buh_line     "phase-1 admin SSH session uses PreferredAuthentications="
  buh_line     "publickey,password, falls through to a /dev/tty password prompt"
  buh_line     "on a fresh node, the operator types the admin password once,"
  buh_line     "fenestrate installs the admin pubkey + sshd hardening + restarts"
  buh_line     "sshd, and phase 2 reconnects by key alone. Subsequent fenestrate"
  buh_line     "and garrison runs use key auth automatically. The operator's"
  buh_line     "manual scope on Windows reduces to: make sshd reachable on the"
  buh_line     "network with a known admin password."
  buh_e
  buh_line     "On Linux/Mac there is no fenestrate verb; admin trust is"
  buh_line     "operator-manual (ssh-copy-id or equivalent). Garrison runs"
  buh_line     "directly against an existing key-trusted admin foothold."
}

zbuhj_render_linux_mac_note() {
  buh_section  "Linux and macOS"
  buh_line     "sshd is typically already installed and reachable. The operator's"
  buh_line     "manual scope is just: confirm sshd is running and the host is"
  buh_line     "reachable from the station. Garrison handles admin pubkey"
  buh_line     "placement and sshd hardening on first run via the password-"
  buh_line     "fallback path."
  buh_e
  buh_line     "If you are unsure of the admin user's password (or it has none),"
  buh_line     "set it to a known value first; garrison clears the password-"
  buh_line     "fallback path on first run, so the value stops affecting SSH:"
  buh_e
  buh_code     "sudo passwd <admin-user>"
  buh_e
  buh_line     "Operators who prefer to pre-establish key trust manually may use"
  buh_line     "the standard recipe instead; garrison's first run then converges"
  buh_line     "as a no-op on the admin pubkey:"
  buh_e
  buh_code     "ssh-copy-id -i ~/.ssh/<admin-pubkey>.pub <admin-user>@<host>"
}

zbuhj_render_windows_bootstrap() {
  buh_section  "Windows: sshd Reachability"
  buh_line     "All steps run on the Windows host in an elevated PowerShell."
  buh_line     "Right-click Start, Terminal (Admin), or search 'PowerShell' and"
  buh_line     "Run as Administrator."
  buh_e
  buh_section  "Preconditions:"
  buh_line     "- Windows host with administrator access"
  buh_line     "- Network reachable on TCP/${BUBC_windows_ssh_port} from operator's station"
  buh_e

  buh_step1    "Set or Confirm Admin Password:"
  buh_line     "Garrison's first run authenticates as the admin user via password"
  buh_line     "(once) before installing the pubkey. If you already know your local"
  buh_line     "admin password, skip. Otherwise set it to a known value:"
  buh_code     "net user <admin-user> <temp-password>"
  buh_line     "After garrison runs, sshd is hardened to key-only and this value"
  buh_line     "stops affecting SSH (it remains your Windows logon password — leave"
  buh_line     "it or reset to taste)."
  buh_e

  buh_step1    "Install OpenSSH Server:"
  buh_line     "Downloads from Windows Update; may take 10+ minutes."
  buh_code     "Add-WindowsCapability -Online -Name OpenSSH.Server~~~~0.0.1.0"
  buh_e

  buh_step1    "Start and Enable the Service:"
  buh_line     "First start also generates the default sshd_config."
  buh_code     "Start-Service sshd"
  buh_code     "Set-Service -Name sshd -StartupType Automatic"
  buh_e

  buyy_cmd_yawp "${BUBC_windows_sshd_config}"; local -r z_sshd_config_yelp="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buh_step1    "Enable Password Authentication in sshd_config:"
  buh_line     "Windows OpenSSH ships with sshd_config in a state that blocks"
  buh_line     "garrison's first-run password fallback (the wire protocol"
  buh_line     "advertises keyboard-interactive but the server refuses to"
  buh_line     "actually prompt). Open sshd_config, find the"
  buh_line     "PasswordAuthentication directive (commented or otherwise),"
  buh_line     "and set it to exactly:"
  buh_e
  buh_code     "PasswordAuthentication yes"
  buh_e
  buh_line     "If no PasswordAuthentication line exists, add one. Save"
  buh_line     "(notepad inherits your elevated context so SYSTEM-owned writes"
  buh_line     "succeed). Garrison flips this back to 'no' as part of its"
  buh_line     "hardening step, so this is a temporary state."
  buh_line     "File: ${z_sshd_config_yelp}"
  buh_e
  buh_code     "notepad ${BUBC_windows_sshd_config}"
  buh_code     "Restart-Service sshd"
  buh_e

  buh_step1    "Allow Port ${BUBC_windows_ssh_port} Through Firewall:"
  buh_code     "New-NetFirewallRule -Name ${BUBC_windows_fw_rule_name} -DisplayName \"${BUBC_windows_fw_display_name}\" -Enabled True -Direction Inbound -Protocol TCP -Action Allow -LocalPort ${BUBC_windows_ssh_port}"
  buh_e

  buh_section  "Verify Reachability"
  buh_line     "From the operator's station, confirm sshd answers (a password"
  buh_line     "prompt is expected and correct — garrison's first run will"
  buh_line     "type it through to ssh on /dev/tty):"
  buh_e
  buh_code     "ssh <admin-user>@<windows-host>"
}

zbuhj_render_post_bootstrap() {
  buh_section  "Run Garrison"
  buh_line     "With sshd reachable, run garrison for the chosen workload shell."
  buh_line     "Garrison establishes admin trust on its first run (typing the"
  buh_line     "admin password once when ssh prompts on /dev/tty), then destroys"
  buh_line     "any prior workload account and provisions a fresh one named"
  buh_line     "BURC_WORKLOAD_USER. Subsequent runs use key auth automatically."
  buh_e
  buh_line     "  buw-jpgb <investiture>  — native bash workload (Linux, macOS)"
  buh_line     "  buw-jpgc <investiture>  — Cygwin bash workload (Windows)"
  buh_line     "  buw-jpgw <investiture>  — WSL bash workload (Windows)"
  buh_e
  buh_line     "Choose c or w for Windows by which workload runtime you need."
  buh_line     "On Windows, both share this same admin SSH bootstrap; the"
  buh_line     "shell-letter only routes garrison's own admin session."
  buh_e
  buh_line     "After garrison succeeds, dispatch work via the workload"
  buh_line     "tabtargets:"
  buh_line     "  buw-jwk <investiture>           — probe workload reachability"
  buh_line     "  buw-jwc <investiture> <file>    — run a command file"
  buh_line     "  buw-jws <investiture>           — interactive workload session"
}

######################################################################
# External Functions (buhj_*)

buhj_top() {
  zbuhj_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Display jurisdiction handbook landing + sshd-reachability procedures"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  zbuhj_render_landing
  buh_e
  zbuhj_render_linux_mac_note
  buh_e
  zbuhj_render_windows_bootstrap
  buh_e
  zbuhj_render_post_bootstrap
}

# eof

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Bash Utility Handbook - Jurisdiction Procedures
#
# Handbook content for BUK's remote-node feature area (BUS0 §Remote
# Node Access). On Windows, fenestrate handles first-run admin trust
# establishment and sshd_config hardening (password-fallback on
# /dev/tty during its phase-1 admin session, then key-only
# thereafter); garrison handles workload account provisioning. The
# operator's manual scope is just making sshd reachable on the
# network with a known admin password. Linux/Mac admin trust is
# operator-manual (e.g., ssh-copy-id); BUK ships no fenestrate for
# non-Windows-OpenSSH nodes.

set -euo pipefail

test -z "${ZBUHJ_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module buhj multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZBUHJ_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Internal: Kindle and Sentinel

zbuhj_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZBUHJ_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module buhj already kindled"

  test -n "${BUBC_windows_ssh_port:-}" || buc_die "buhj requires bubc_constants.sh sourced before kindle"

  readonly ZBUHJ_KINDLED=1
}

zbuhj_sentinel() {
  test "${ZBUHJ_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module buhj not kindled - call zbuhj_kindle first"
}

######################################################################
# Internal: Section renderers

zbuhj_render_landing() {
  buh_section  "Jurisdiction Handbook"
  buh_line     "BUK reaches remote nodes through two ceremonies. Fenestrate"
  buh_line     "(Windows OpenSSH only) hardens admin SSH trust and sshd_config"
  buh_line     "to key-only; Garrison provisions the workload account. BUK"
  buh_line     "never generates or modifies SSH key material; the operator owns"
  buh_line     "all key administration."
  buh_e
  buh_line     "On Windows, fenestrate handles first-run admin trust itself: its"
  buh_line     "phase-1 admin SSH session uses PreferredAuthentications="
  buh_line     "publickey,password, falls through to a /dev/tty password prompt"
  buh_line     "on a fresh node, the operator types the admin password once,"
  buh_line     "fenestrate installs the admin pubkey + sshd hardening + restarts"
  buh_line     "sshd, and phase 2 reconnects by key alone. Subsequent fenestrate"
  buh_line     "and garrison runs use key auth automatically. The operator's"
  buh_line     "manual scope on Windows reduces to: make sshd reachable on the"
  buh_line     "network with a known admin password."
  buh_e
  buh_line     "On Linux/Mac there is no fenestrate verb; admin trust is"
  buh_line     "operator-manual (ssh-copy-id or equivalent). Garrison runs"
  buh_line     "directly against an existing key-trusted admin foothold."
}

zbuhj_render_linux_mac_note() {
  buh_section  "Linux and macOS"
  buh_line     "sshd is typically already installed and reachable. The operator's"
  buh_line     "manual scope is: confirm sshd is running and the host is reachable,"
  buh_line     "then place the admin pubkey via ssh-copy-id (or equivalent) so"
  buh_line     "garrison's first admin SSH succeeds by key alone. There is no"
  buh_line     "fenestrate verb for non-Windows-OpenSSH nodes; sshd hardening is"
  buh_line     "operator-managed."
  buh_e
  buh_code     "ssh-copy-id -i ~/.ssh/<admin-pubkey>.pub <admin-user>@<host>"
}

zbuhj_render_windows_bootstrap() {
  buh_section  "Windows: sshd Reachability"
  buh_line     "All steps run on the Windows host in an elevated PowerShell."
  buh_line     "Right-click Start, Terminal (Admin), or search 'PowerShell' and"
  buh_line     "Run as Administrator."
  buh_e
  buh_section  "Preconditions:"
  buh_line     "- Windows host with administrator access"
  buh_line     "- Network reachable on TCP/${BUBC_windows_ssh_port} from operator's station"
  buh_e

  buh_step1    "Set or Confirm Admin Password:"
  buh_line     "Fenestrate's phase-1 admin SSH session authenticates via password"
  buh_line     "(once) before installing the pubkey. If you already know your local"
  buh_line     "admin password, skip. Otherwise set it to a known value:"
  buh_code     "net user <admin-user> <temp-password>"
  buh_line     "After fenestrate runs, sshd is hardened to key-only and this value"
  buh_line     "stops affecting SSH (it remains your Windows logon password — leave"
  buh_line     "it or reset to taste)."
  buh_e

  buh_step1    "Install OpenSSH Server:"
  buh_line     "Downloads from Windows Update; may take 10+ minutes."
  buh_code     "Add-WindowsCapability -Online -Name OpenSSH.Server~~~~0.0.1.0"
  buh_e

  buh_step1    "Start and Enable the Service:"
  buh_line     "First start also generates the default sshd_config."
  buh_code     "Start-Service sshd"
  buh_code     "Set-Service -Name sshd -StartupType Automatic"
  buh_e

  buyy_cmd_yawp "${BUBC_windows_sshd_config}"; local -r z_sshd_config_yelp="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buh_step1    "Enable Password Authentication in sshd_config:"
  buh_line     "Windows OpenSSH ships with sshd_config in a state that blocks"
  buh_line     "garrison's first-run password fallback (the wire protocol"
  buh_line     "advertises keyboard-interactive but the server refuses to"
  buh_line     "actually prompt). Open sshd_config, find the"
  buh_line     "PasswordAuthentication directive (commented or otherwise),"
  buh_line     "and set it to exactly:"
  buh_e
  buh_code     "PasswordAuthentication yes"
  buh_e
  buh_line     "If no PasswordAuthentication line exists, add one. Save"
  buh_line     "(notepad inherits your elevated context so SYSTEM-owned writes"
  buh_line     "succeed). Garrison flips this back to 'no' as part of its"
  buh_line     "hardening step, so this is a temporary state."
  buh_line     "File: ${z_sshd_config_yelp}"
  buh_e
  buh_code     "notepad ${BUBC_windows_sshd_config}"
  buh_code     "Restart-Service sshd"
  buh_e

  buh_step1    "Allow Port ${BUBC_windows_ssh_port} Through Firewall:"
  buh_code     "New-NetFirewallRule -Name ${BUBC_windows_fw_rule_name} -DisplayName \"${BUBC_windows_fw_display_name}\" -Enabled True -Direction Inbound -Protocol TCP -Action Allow -LocalPort ${BUBC_windows_ssh_port}"
  buh_e

  buh_section  "Verify Reachability"
  buh_line     "From the operator's station, confirm sshd answers (a password"
  buh_line     "prompt is expected and correct — garrison's first run will"
  buh_line     "type it through to ssh on /dev/tty):"
  buh_e
  buh_code     "ssh <admin-user>@<windows-host>"
}

zbuhj_render_post_bootstrap() {
  buh_section  "Run Garrison"
  buh_line     "With sshd reachable, run garrison for the chosen workload shell."
  buh_line     "Garrison establishes admin trust on its first run (typing the"
  buh_line     "admin password once when ssh prompts on /dev/tty), then destroys"
  buh_line     "any prior workload account and provisions a fresh one named"
  buh_line     "BURC_WORKLOAD_USER. Subsequent runs use key auth automatically."
  buh_e
  buh_line     "  buw-jpgb <investiture>  — native bash workload (Linux, macOS)"
  buh_line     "  buw-jpgc <investiture>  — Cygwin bash workload (Windows)"
  buh_line     "  buw-jpgw <investiture>  — WSL bash workload (Windows)"
  buh_e
  buh_line     "Choose c or w for Windows by which workload runtime you need."
  buh_line     "On Windows, both share this same admin SSH bootstrap; the"
  buh_line     "shell-letter only routes garrison's own admin session."
  buh_e
  buh_line     "After garrison succeeds, dispatch work via the workload"
  buh_line     "tabtargets:"
  buh_line     "  buw-jwk <investiture>           — probe workload reachability"
  buh_line     "  buw-jwc <investiture> <file>    — run a command file"
  buh_line     "  buw-jws <investiture>           — interactive workload session"
}

######################################################################
# External Functions (buhj_*)

buhj_top() {
  zbuhj_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Display jurisdiction handbook landing + sshd-reachability procedures"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  zbuhj_render_landing
  buh_e
  zbuhj_render_linux_mac_note
  buh_e
  zbuhj_render_windows_bootstrap
  buh_e
  zbuhj_render_post_bootstrap
}

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Bash Utility Handbook - Jurisdiction Procedures
#
# Handbook content for BUK's remote-node feature area (BUS0 §Remote
# Node Access). On Windows, fenestrate handles first-run admin trust
# establishment and sshd_config hardening (password-fallback on
# /dev/tty during its phase-1 admin session, then key-only
# thereafter); garrison handles workload account provisioning. The
# operator's manual scope is just making sshd reachable on the
# network with a known admin password. Linux/Mac admin trust is
# operator-manual (e.g., ssh-copy-id); BUK ships no fenestrate for
# non-Windows-OpenSSH nodes.

set -euo pipefail

test -z "${ZBUHJ_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module buhj multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZBUHJ_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Internal: Kindle and Sentinel

zbuhj_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZBUHJ_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module buhj already kindled"

  test -n "${BUBC_windows_ssh_port:-}" || buc_die "buhj requires bubc_constants.sh sourced before kindle"

  readonly ZBUHJ_KINDLED=1
}

zbuhj_sentinel() {
  test "${ZBUHJ_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module buhj not kindled - call zbuhj_kindle first"
}

######################################################################
# Internal: Section renderers

zbuhj_render_landing() {
  buh_section  "Jurisdiction Handbook"
  buh_line     "BUK reaches remote nodes through two ceremonies. Fenestrate"
  buh_line     "(Windows OpenSSH only) hardens admin SSH trust and sshd_config"
  buh_line     "to key-only; Garrison provisions the workload account. BUK"
  buh_line     "never generates or modifies SSH key material; the operator owns"
  buh_line     "all key administration."
  buh_e
  buh_line     "On Windows, fenestrate handles first-run admin trust itself: its"
  buh_line     "phase-1 admin SSH session uses PreferredAuthentications="
  buh_line     "publickey,password, falls through to a /dev/tty password prompt"
  buh_line     "on a fresh node, the operator types the admin password once,"
  buh_line     "fenestrate installs the admin pubkey + sshd hardening + restarts"
  buh_line     "sshd, and phase 2 reconnects by key alone. Subsequent fenestrate"
  buh_line     "and garrison runs use key auth automatically. The operator's"
  buh_line     "manual scope on Windows reduces to: make sshd reachable on the"
  buh_line     "network with a known admin password."
  buh_e
  buh_line     "On Linux/Mac there is no fenestrate verb; admin trust is"
  buh_line     "operator-manual (ssh-copy-id or equivalent). Garrison runs"
  buh_line     "directly against an existing key-trusted admin foothold."
}

zbuhj_render_linux_mac_note() {
  buh_section  "Linux and macOS"
  buh_line     "sshd is typically already installed and reachable. The operator's"
  buh_line     "manual scope is: confirm sshd is running and the host is reachable,"
  buh_line     "then place the admin pubkey via ssh-copy-id (or equivalent) so"
  buh_line     "garrison's first admin SSH succeeds by key alone. There is no"
  buh_line     "fenestrate verb for non-Windows-OpenSSH nodes; sshd hardening is"
  buh_line     "operator-managed."
  buh_e
  buh_code     "ssh-copy-id -i ~/.ssh/<admin-pubkey>.pub <admin-user>@<host>"
}

zbuhj_render_windows_bootstrap() {
  buh_section  "Windows: sshd Reachability"
  buh_line     "All steps run on the Windows host in an elevated PowerShell."
  buh_line     "Right-click Start, Terminal (Admin), or search 'PowerShell' and"
  buh_line     "Run as Administrator."
  buh_e
  buh_section  "Preconditions:"
  buh_line     "- Windows host with administrator access"
  buh_line     "- Network reachable on TCP/${BUBC_windows_ssh_port} from operator's station"
  buh_e

  buh_step1    "Set or Confirm Admin Password:"
  buh_line     "Fenestrate's phase-1 admin SSH session authenticates via password"
  buh_line     "(once) before installing the pubkey. If you already know your local"
  buh_line     "admin password, skip. Otherwise set it to a known value:"
  buh_code     "net user <admin-user> <temp-password>"
  buh_line     "After fenestrate runs, sshd is hardened to key-only and this value"
  buh_line     "stops affecting SSH (it remains your Windows logon password — leave"
  buh_line     "it or reset to taste)."
  buh_e

  buh_step1    "Install OpenSSH Server:"
  buh_line     "Downloads from Windows Update; may take 10+ minutes."
  buh_code     "Add-WindowsCapability -Online -Name OpenSSH.Server~~~~0.0.1.0"
  buh_e

  buh_step1    "Start and Enable the Service:"
  buh_line     "First start also generates the default sshd_config."
  buh_code     "Start-Service sshd"
  buh_code     "Set-Service -Name sshd -StartupType Automatic"
  buh_e

  buyy_cmd_yawp "${BUBC_windows_sshd_config}"; local -r z_sshd_config_yelp="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buh_step1    "Enable Password Authentication in sshd_config:"
  buh_line     "Windows OpenSSH ships with sshd_config in a state that blocks"
  buh_line     "fenestrate's phase-1 password fallback (the wire protocol"
  buh_line     "advertises keyboard-interactive but the server refuses to"
  buh_line     "actually prompt). Open sshd_config, find the"
  buh_line     "PasswordAuthentication directive (commented or otherwise),"
  buh_line     "and set it to exactly:"
  buh_e
  buh_code     "PasswordAuthentication yes"
  buh_e
  buh_line     "If no PasswordAuthentication line exists, add one. Save"
  buh_line     "(notepad inherits your elevated context so SYSTEM-owned writes"
  buh_line     "succeed). Fenestrate flips this back to 'no' as part of its"
  buh_line     "hardening step, so this is a temporary state."
  buh_line     "File: ${z_sshd_config_yelp}"
  buh_e
  buh_code     "notepad ${BUBC_windows_sshd_config}"
  buh_code     "Restart-Service sshd"
  buh_e

  buh_step1    "Allow Port ${BUBC_windows_ssh_port} Through Firewall:"
  buh_code     "New-NetFirewallRule -Name ${BUBC_windows_fw_rule_name} -DisplayName \"${BUBC_windows_fw_display_name}\" -Enabled True -Direction Inbound -Protocol TCP -Action Allow -LocalPort ${BUBC_windows_ssh_port}"
  buh_e

  buh_section  "Verify Reachability"
  buh_line     "From the operator's station, confirm sshd answers (a password"
  buh_line     "prompt is expected and correct — fenestrate's phase-1 admin"
  buh_line     "session will type it through to ssh on /dev/tty):"
  buh_e
  buh_code     "ssh <admin-user>@<windows-host>"
}

zbuhj_render_post_bootstrap() {
  buh_section  "Run Garrison"
  buh_line     "With sshd reachable, run garrison for the chosen workload shell."
  buh_line     "Garrison establishes admin trust on its first run (typing the"
  buh_line     "admin password once when ssh prompts on /dev/tty), then destroys"
  buh_line     "any prior workload account and provisions a fresh one named"
  buh_line     "BURC_WORKLOAD_USER. Subsequent runs use key auth automatically."
  buh_e
  buh_line     "  buw-jpgb <investiture>  — native bash workload (Linux, macOS)"
  buh_line     "  buw-jpgc <investiture>  — Cygwin bash workload (Windows)"
  buh_line     "  buw-jpgw <investiture>  — WSL bash workload (Windows)"
  buh_e
  buh_line     "Choose c or w for Windows by which workload runtime you need."
  buh_line     "On Windows, both share this same admin SSH bootstrap; the"
  buh_line     "shell-letter only routes garrison's own admin session."
  buh_e
  buh_line     "After garrison succeeds, dispatch work via the workload"
  buh_line     "tabtargets:"
  buh_line     "  buw-jwk <investiture>           — probe workload reachability"
  buh_line     "  buw-jwc <investiture> <file>    — run a command file"
  buh_line     "  buw-jws <investiture>           — interactive workload session"
}

######################################################################
# External Functions (buhj_*)

buhj_top() {
  zbuhj_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Display jurisdiction handbook landing + sshd-reachability procedures"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  zbuhj_render_landing
  buh_e
  zbuhj_render_linux_mac_note
  buh_e
  zbuhj_render_windows_bootstrap
  buh_e
  zbuhj_render_post_bootstrap
}
